    # Prefer a genuine string/ISO parse; fall back to numeric epoch interpretation.
    return F.coalesce(F.to_timestamp(raw.cast("string")), ts_from_num)


def main():
    spark = (
        SparkSession.builder.appName("p10_scd2")
        .config("spark.sql.session.timeZone", "UTC")
        .getOrCreate()
    )
    spark.sparkContext.setLogLevel("WARN")

    inp = os.environ.get("AGENT_INPUT_PATH", DEFAULT_INPUT)
    out_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH", DEFAULT_OUTPUT)

    df = spark.read.json(inp)
    cols = df.columns

    # ---- schema detection (feeds are messy / not controlled by us) ----------
    id_c = pick(cols, ["customer_id", "cust_id", "customerId", "id", "customerid"])
    tier_c = pick(cols, ["tier", "tier_name", "customer_tier", "level"])
    region_c = pick(cols, ["region", "region_name", "geo", "country", "location"])
    ts_c = pick(
        cols,
        [
            "event_time", "event_ts", "event_timestamp", "updated_at", "update_ts",
            "change_ts", "changed_at", "ts", "timestamp", "modified_at",
            "effective_ts", "effective_from", "valid_from", "commit_ts", "op_ts",
        ],
    )
    seq_c = pick(
        cols,
        ["seq", "sequence", "__seq", "offset", "lsn", "version", "change_seq",
         "event_id", "_seq", "scn", "commit_seq"],
    )
    op_c = pick(cols, ["op", "operation", "_op", "change_type", "cdc_op", "action", "__op", "optype"])

    if id_c is None or ts_c is None:
        raise ValueError(
            "Could not detect required id/timestamp columns. Present: %s" % cols
        )

    tier_col = F.col(tier_c).cast("string") if tier_c else F.lit(None).cast("string")
    region_col = F.col(region_c).cast("string") if region_c else F.lit(None).cast("string")
    seq_col = F.col(seq_c).cast("double") if seq_c else F.lit(None).cast("double")

    if op_c:
        opnorm = F.lower(F.trim(F.col(op_c).cast("string")))
        is_deleted = opnorm.isin("d", "del", "delete", "deleted", "r_delete")
    else:
        is_deleted = F.lit(False)

    # Total-order tie-break: stable hash of the entire raw row.
    tb = F.crc32(
        F.concat_ws("|", *[F.coalesce(F.col(c).cast("string"), F.lit("\u0000")) for c in cols])
    )

    base = (
        df.select(
            F.col(id_c).cast("string").alias("customer_id"),
            tier_col.alias("tier"),
            region_col.alias("region"),
            build_timestamp(ts_c).alias("ts"),
            seq_col.alias("seq"),
            is_deleted.cast("boolean").alias("is_deleted"),
            tb.alias("tb"),
        )
        .where(F.col("customer_id").isNotNull() & F.col("ts").isNotNull())
    )

    # ---- 1) collapse same-(customer, ts) contradictions deterministically ----
    # Highest seq wins (latest logical change at that instant); tie-break by hash.
    wdd = Window.partitionBy("customer_id", "ts").orderBy(
        F.col("seq").desc_nulls_last(), F.col("tb").desc()
    )
    dedup = (
        base.withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(wdd))
        .where(F.col("rn") == 1)
        .drop("rn", "tb")
    )

    # ---- 2) fold consecutive no-op states (only keep REAL changes) -----------
    worder = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(F.col("ts").asc())
    prev_tier = F.lag("tier").over(worder)
    prev_region = F.lag("region").over(worder)
    prev_del = F.lag("is_deleted").over(worder)
    prev_ts = F.lag("ts").over(worder)

    changed = prev_ts.isNull() | ~(
        F.col("tier").eqNullSafe(prev_tier)
        & F.col("region").eqNullSafe(prev_region)
        & F.col("is_deleted").eqNullSafe(prev_del)
    )
    states = dedup.withColumn("_changed", changed).where(F.col("_changed")).drop("_changed")

    # ---- 3) build non-overlapping validity periods ---------------------------
    wp = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(F.col("ts").asc())
    next_ts = F.lead("ts").over(wp)

    history = (
        states.withColumn("valid_from", F.col("ts"))
        .withColumn("valid_to", next_ts)  # == next version's valid_from -> no overlap
        .withColumn("is_current", F.col("valid_to").isNull())
    )

    history_out = history.select(
        "customer_id", "tier", "region", "valid_from", "valid_to", "is_current"
    ).orderBy("customer_id", "valid_from")

    # ---- 4) current state: latest real change, deletions removed -------------
    current_out = (
        history.where(F.col("is_current") & (~F.col("is_deleted")))
        .select("customer_id", "tier", "region")
        .orderBy("customer_id")
    )

    # Register contract-named temp views in this SparkSession.
    history_out.createOrReplaceTempView("customers_history")
    current_out.createOrReplaceTempView("customers_current")

    # Materialize GOLD current-state to the contract parquet location.
    current_out.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(out_path)

    # Durably persist history alongside for reconciliation/inspection.
    hist_path = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(out_path.rstrip("/")), "customers_history.parquet")
    try:
        history_out.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(hist_path)
    except Exception as e:  # non-fatal: temp view already satisfies the contract
        sys.stderr.write("history parquet write skipped: %s\n" % e)

    print("Run is COMPLETED")
    spark.stop()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window
from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, StringType

DATA_PATH = "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_customers_cdc_seed42.ndjson"

# Superset schema: every plausible field name declared as string.
# With an explicit schema the JSON reader is permissive — absent fields become
# null, extra fields are ignored, and numeric tokens are read as their text.
_CANDIDATE_FIELDS = [
    "customer_id", "id", "cust_id", "customerId",
    "tier", "customer_tier", "new_tier",
    "region", "customer_region", "new_region",
    "ts", "event_ts", "updated_at", "change_ts", "event_time",
    "timestamp", "ts_ms", "valid_from", "modified_at", "_ts",
    "seq", "sequence", "offset", "_seq", "version", "lsn", "scn",
    "op", "change_type",
]
_SCHEMA = StructType([StructField(f, StringType(), True) for f in _CANDIDATE_FIELDS])


def _first(*cands):
    return F.coalesce(*[F.col(c) for c in cands])


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_cdc_raw")
def customers_cdc_raw():
    # Explicit schema => no schema inference (no analysis action), fully lazy.
    return SparkSession.active().read.schema(_SCHEMA).json(DATA_PATH)


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_history")
def customers_history():
    src = SparkSession.active().read.table("spark_catalog.default.customers_cdc_raw")

    cust = _first("customer_id", "id", "cust_id", "customerId").cast("string")
    tier = _first("tier", "customer_tier", "new_tier").cast("string")
    region = _first("region", "customer_region", "new_region").cast("string")

    ts_str = _first(
        "ts", "event_ts", "updated_at", "change_ts", "event_time",
        "timestamp", "ts_ms", "valid_from", "modified_at", "_ts",
    ).cast("string")
    # Parse either an ISO-8601 string OR an epoch (seconds/millis) numeric string,
    # yielding a monotonic, deterministic timestamp regardless of source encoding.
    order_ts = F.coalesce(
        F.to_timestamp(ts_str),
        F.timestamp_seconds(ts_str.cast("long")),
    )

    seq_val = F.coalesce(
        _first("seq", "sequence", "offset", "_seq", "version", "lsn", "scn").cast("long"),
        F.lit(0).cast("long"),
    )

    norm = (
        src.select(
            cust.alias("customer_id"),
            tier.alias("tier"),
            region.alias("region"),
            order_ts.alias("event_ts"),
            seq_val.alias("seq_val"),
            ts_str.alias("raw_ts"),
        )
        .where(F.col("customer_id").isNotNull())
    )

    # Deterministic ordering per customer; tiebreakers keep reprocessing stable.
    w = (
        Window.partitionBy("customer_id")
        .orderBy(
            F.col("event_ts").asc_nulls_last(),
            F.col("seq_val").asc_nulls_last(),
            F.col("raw_ts").asc_nulls_last(),
            F.col("tier").asc_nulls_last(),
            F.col("region").asc_nulls_last(),
        )
    )

    ranked = (
        norm.withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(w))
        .withColumn("prev_tier", F.lag("tier").over(w))
        .withColumn("prev_region", F.lag("region").over(w))
    )

    # Collapse consecutive-identical states -> one version per real change.
    starts = ranked.where(
        (F.col("rn") == 1)
        | (~F.col("tier").eqNullSafe(F.col("prev_tier")))
        | (~F.col("region").eqNullSafe(F.col("prev_region")))
    )

    # Non-overlapping periods: each version ends where the next begins.
    w2 = (
        Window.partitionBy("customer_id")
        .orderBy(F.col("event_ts").asc_nulls_last(), F.col("seq_val").asc_nulls_last())
    )

    history = starts.select(
        F.col("customer_id"),
        F.col("tier"),
        F.col("region"),
        F.col("event_ts").alias("valid_from"),
        F.lead("event_ts").over(w2).alias("valid_to"),
    ).withColumn("is_current", F.col("valid_to").isNull())

    return history.select(
        "customer_id", "tier", "region", "valid_from", "valid_to", "is_current"
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_current")
def customers_current():
    hist = SparkSession.active().read.table("spark_catalog.default.customers_history")
    return (
        hist.where(F.col("is_current"))
        .select("customer_id", "tier", "region")
    )

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window
from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, StringType, LongType

SRC = ("file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/"
       ".work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_customers_cdc_seed1337.ndjson")

# Explicit superset schema: missing fields -> null (no inference job, fully lazy).
SCHEMA = StructType([
    StructField("customer_id", StringType()),
    StructField("cust_id", StringType()),
    StructField("id", StringType()),
    StructField("tier", StringType()),
    StructField("region", StringType()),
    StructField("op", StringType()),
    StructField("operation", StringType()),
    StructField("ts", StringType()),
    StructField("event_time", StringType()),
    StructField("event_ts", StringType()),
    StructField("updated_at", StringType()),
    StructField("change_ts", StringType()),
    StructField("timestamp", StringType()),
    StructField("seq", LongType()),
    StructField("sequence", LongType()),
    StructField("offset", LongType()),
    StructField("lsn", LongType()),
])


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_cdc_norm")
def customers_cdc_norm():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.schema(SCHEMA).json(SRC)
    cid = F.coalesce(F.col("customer_id"), F.col("cust_id"), F.col("id"))
    ts = F.coalesce(F.col("ts"), F.col("event_time"), F.col("event_ts"),
                    F.col("updated_at"), F.col("change_ts"), F.col("timestamp"))
    seq = F.coalesce(F.col("seq"), F.col("sequence"),
                     F.col("offset"), F.col("lsn"))
    op = F.coalesce(F.col("op"), F.col("operation"))
    # Stable tiebreak so contradictory same-instant events resolve deterministically.
    rowhash = F.sha2(F.concat_ws("|",
                                 F.coalesce(cid, F.lit("")),
                                 F.coalesce(F.col("tier"), F.lit("")),
                                 F.coalesce(F.col("region"), F.lit("")),
                                 F.coalesce(op, F.lit("")),
                                 F.coalesce(ts, F.lit("")),
                                 F.coalesce(seq.cast("string"), F.lit(""))), 256)
    return (df.select(
                cid.alias("customer_id"),
                F.col("tier"),
                F.col("region"),
                op.alias("op"),
                F.to_timestamp(ts).alias("event_ts"),
                seq.alias("seq"),
                rowhash.alias("rowhash"))
              .where(cid.isNotNull()))


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_history")
def customers_history():
    src = SparkSession.active().read.table("spark_catalog.default.customers_cdc_norm")
    w = (Window.partitionBy("customer_id")
               .orderBy(F.col("event_ts").asc_nulls_last(),
                        F.col("seq").asc_nulls_last(),
                        F.col("rowhash").asc()))
    ranked = (src.withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(w))
                 .withColumn("prev_tier", F.lag("tier").over(w))
                 .withColumn("prev_region", F.lag("region").over(w)))
    # Keep only real changes (collapse consecutive identical tier/region).
    changed = ranked.where(
        (F.col("rn") == 1) |
        (~(F.col("tier").eqNullSafe(F.col("prev_tier")) &
           F.col("region").eqNullSafe(F.col("prev_region")))))
    w2 = (Window.partitionBy("customer_id")
                .orderBy(F.col("event_ts").asc_nulls_last(),
                         F.col("seq").asc_nulls_last(),
                         F.col("rowhash").asc()))
    hist = (changed
            .withColumn("valid_from", F.col("event_ts"))
            .withColumn("valid_to", F.lead("event_ts").over(w2))
            .withColumn("is_current", F.col("valid_to").isNull()))
    return hist.select("customer_id", "tier", "region",
                       "valid_from", "valid_to", "is_current")


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_current")
def customers_current():
    hist = SparkSession.active().read.table("spark_catalog.default.customers_history")
    return (hist.where(F.col("is_current"))
                .select("customer_id", "tier", "region"))

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window

DATA_PATH = "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_customers_cdc_seed2718.ndjson"

# Fixed schema -> no schema-inference action, no missing-column analysis errors.
# customer_id / tier / region are guaranteed by the contract; the rest are
# candidate names for the change timestamp and an optional ordering sequence.
_SCHEMA = (
    "customer_id STRING, tier STRING, region STRING, "
    "ts STRING, event_ts STRING, event_time STRING, event_timestamp STRING, "
    "updated_at STRING, created_at STRING, change_ts STRING, effective_ts STRING, "
    "valid_from STRING, op STRING, "
    "seq LONG, sequence LONG, version LONG, lsn LONG"
)


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_scd_events")
def customers_scd_events():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    raw = spark.read.text(DATA_PATH)
    parsed = raw.select(F.from_json(F.col("value"), _SCHEMA).alias("j")).select("j.*")

    et = F.coalesce(
        F.col("ts"), F.col("event_ts"), F.col("event_time"), F.col("event_timestamp"),
        F.col("updated_at"), F.col("created_at"), F.col("change_ts"),
        F.col("effective_ts"), F.col("valid_from"),
    )
    order_seq = F.coalesce(
        F.col("seq"), F.col("sequence"), F.col("version"), F.col("lsn"),
    )

    # Robust to ISO strings or epoch-second numeric strings.
    ts_parsed = F.coalesce(
        F.to_timestamp(et),
        F.to_timestamp(F.from_unixtime(et.cast("double").cast("long"))),
    )

    return (
        parsed
        .select(
            F.col("customer_id").cast("string").alias("customer_id"),
            F.col("tier").cast("string").alias("tier"),
            F.col("region").cast("string").alias("region"),
            ts_parsed.alias("event_ts"),
            order_seq.alias("order_seq"),
        )
        .where(F.col("customer_id").isNotNull() & F.col("event_ts").isNotNull())
        .dropDuplicates(["customer_id", "tier", "region", "event_ts", "order_seq"])
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_history")
def customers_history():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    ev = spark.read.table("spark_catalog.default.customers_scd_events")

    w_order = (
        Window.partitionBy("customer_id")
        .orderBy(
            F.col("event_ts").asc_nulls_first(),
            F.col("order_seq").asc_nulls_first(),
            F.col("tier").asc_nulls_first(),
            F.col("region").asc_nulls_first(),
        )
    )
    w_run = w_order.rowsBetween(Window.unboundedPreceding, Window.currentRow)

    prev_tier = F.lag("tier").over(w_order)
    prev_region = F.lag("region").over(w_order)
    is_change = F.when(
        prev_tier.isNull()
        | (~prev_tier.eqNullSafe(F.col("tier")))
        | (~prev_region.eqNullSafe(F.col("region"))),
        F.lit(1),
    ).otherwise(F.lit(0))

    tagged = ev.withColumn("grp", F.sum(is_change).over(w_run))

    # Collapse consecutive identical (tier, region) runs into one version.
    periods = tagged.groupBy("customer_id", "grp", "tier", "region").agg(
        F.min("event_ts").alias("valid_from")
    )

    w_per = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(F.col("valid_from").asc())
    next_from = F.lead("valid_from").over(w_per)

    return periods.select(
        F.col("customer_id"),
        F.col("tier"),
        F.col("region"),
        F.col("valid_from"),
        next_from.alias("valid_to"),
        next_from.isNull().alias("is_current"),
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_current")
def customers_current():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    hist = spark.read.table("spark_catalog.default.customers_history")
    return (
        hist.where(F.col("is_current"))
        .select("customer_id", "tier", "region")
    )

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F
from pyspark.sql.window import Window
from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, StringType, LongType

DATA = "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_customers_cdc_seed3141.ndjson"

# Explicit schema => no eager schema inference (avoids ATTEMPT_ANALYSIS at plan build).
# Multiple candidate field names are tolerated: fields absent from the JSON parse as null.
_SCHEMA = StructType([
    StructField("customer_id", StringType()),
    StructField("customerId", StringType()),
    StructField("cust_id", StringType()),
    StructField("id", StringType()),
    StructField("tier", StringType()),
    StructField("region", StringType()),
    StructField("ts", StringType()),
    StructField("event_ts", StringType()),
    StructField("event_time", StringType()),
    StructField("eventTime", StringType()),
    StructField("updated_at", StringType()),
    StructField("timestamp", StringType()),
    StructField("change_ts", StringType()),
    StructField("valid_from", StringType()),
    StructField("op", StringType()),
    StructField("seq", LongType()),
    StructField("sequence", LongType()),
])


def _events(spark):
    df = spark.read.schema(_SCHEMA).json(DATA)

    cust = F.coalesce(F.col("customer_id"), F.col("customerId"),
                      F.col("cust_id"), F.col("id"))
    ts = F.coalesce(F.col("ts"), F.col("event_ts"), F.col("event_time"),
                    F.col("eventTime"), F.col("updated_at"),
                    F.col("timestamp"), F.col("change_ts"), F.col("valid_from"))
    seq = F.coalesce(F.col("seq"), F.col("sequence"), F.lit(0).cast("long"))

    df = df.select(
        cust.cast("string").alias("customer_id"),
        F.trim(F.col("tier").cast("string")).alias("tier"),
        F.trim(F.col("region").cast("string")).alias("region"),
        ts.cast("timestamp").alias("event_ts"),
        seq.alias("seq"),
    )

    # keep only well-formed rows
    df = df.where(F.col("customer_id").isNotNull() & F.col("event_ts").isNotNull())

    # collapse exact duplicate events (idempotent reprocessing)
    df = df.dropDuplicates(["customer_id", "tier", "region", "event_ts", "seq"])

    # resolve contradictory records that share a timestamp -> one deterministic winner
    w0 = Window.partitionBy("customer_id", "event_ts").orderBy(
        F.col("seq").desc(),
        F.col("tier").asc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("region").asc_nulls_last(),
    )
    df = df.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w0)).where(F.col("_rn") == 1).drop("_rn")
    return df


def _history(spark):
    e = _events(spark)

    # detect real state changes, dropping no-op repeats (deterministic order)
    w = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy("event_ts")
    prev_t = F.lag("tier").over(w)
    prev_r = F.lag("region").over(w)
    changed = (
        prev_t.isNull()
        | (~F.col("tier").eqNullSafe(prev_t))
        | (~F.col("region").eqNullSafe(prev_r))
    )
    changes = e.withColumn("_chg", changed).where(F.col("_chg"))

    # half-open validity periods [valid_from, valid_to) => never overlap
    w2 = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy("event_ts")
    valid_to = F.lead("event_ts").over(w2)

    return changes.select(
        "customer_id",
        "tier",
        "region",
        F.col("event_ts").alias("valid_from"),
        valid_to.alias("valid_to"),
        valid_to.isNull().alias("is_current"),
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_history")
def customers_history():
    return _history(SparkSession.active())


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_current")
def customers_current():
    h = _history(SparkSession.active())
    return h.where(F.col("is_current")).select("customer_id", "tier", "region")

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window
from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, StringType, LongType

PATH = "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_customers_cdc_seed5772.ndjson"

# Explicit schema: extra candidate names -> null (no inference => no analysis).
CAND_SCHEMA = StructType([
    StructField("customer_id", StringType()),
    StructField("id", StringType()),
    StructField("cust_id", StringType()),
    StructField("tier", StringType()),
    StructField("region", StringType()),
    StructField("ts", StringType()),
    StructField("event_ts", StringType()),
    StructField("event_time", StringType()),
    StructField("updated_at", StringType()),
    StructField("change_ts", StringType()),
    StructField("timestamp", StringType()),
    StructField("valid_from", StringType()),
    StructField("seq", LongType()),
    StructField("sequence", LongType()),
    StructField("offset", LongType()),
    StructField("version", LongType()),
])


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_history")
def customers_history():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    raw = spark.read.schema(CAND_SCHEMA).json(PATH)

    cid = F.coalesce(F.col("customer_id"), F.col("id"), F.col("cust_id"))
    ets_raw = F.coalesce(
        F.col("ts"), F.col("event_ts"), F.col("event_time"),
        F.col("updated_at"), F.col("change_ts"), F.col("timestamp"),
        F.col("valid_from"),
    )
    ets = F.coalesce(F.to_timestamp(ets_raw), F.timestamp_seconds(ets_raw.cast("long")))
    seq = F.coalesce(F.col("seq"), F.col("sequence"), F.col("offset"),
                     F.col("version"), F.lit(0).cast("long"))

    df = (
        raw.select(
            cid.cast("string").alias("customer_id"),
            F.col("tier").cast("string").alias("tier"),
            F.col("region").cast("string").alias("region"),
            ets.alias("event_ts"),
            seq.alias("seq"),
        )
        .where(F.col("customer_id").isNotNull() & F.col("event_ts").isNotNull())
    )

    # Idempotent dedup of identical CDC rows.
    df = df.dropDuplicates(["customer_id", "event_ts", "seq", "tier", "region"])

    # Deterministic per-customer ordering.
    w = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy("event_ts", "seq", "tier", "region")

    prev_tier = F.lag("tier").over(w)
    prev_region = F.lag("region").over(w)
    changed = ~(F.col("tier").eqNullSafe(prev_tier) & F.col("region").eqNullSafe(prev_region))

    changes = df.withColumn("_changed", changed).where(F.col("_changed"))

    w2 = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy("event_ts", "seq", "tier", "region")
    nxt = F.lead("event_ts").over(w2)

    hist = (
        changes
        .withColumn("valid_from", F.col("event_ts"))
        .withColumn("valid_to", nxt)                 # exclusive; null => open/current
        .withColumn("is_current", nxt.isNull())
        .select("customer_id", "tier", "region", "valid_from", "valid_to", "is_current")
    )
    return hist


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_current")
def customers_current():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    h = spark.read.table("spark_catalog.default.customers_history")
    return h.where(F.col("is_current")).select("customer_id", "tier", "region")

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window

PATH = "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_customers_cdc_seed8675.ndjson"

# Explicit schema => NO inference => no analysis/action inside the query function.
# All candidate keys declared as strings (missing keys simply read as NULL).
_SCHEMA = (
    "customer_id string, id string, cust_id string, "
    "tier string, region string, "
    "ts string, event_ts string, event_time string, updated_at string, "
    "change_ts string, effective_ts string, created_at string, "
    "seq long, seqno long, lsn long"
)


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_cdc_raw")
def customers_cdc_raw():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    df = spark.read.schema(_SCHEMA).json(PATH)  # lazy: explicit schema, no read
    return df.select(
        F.coalesce("customer_id", "id", "cust_id").alias("customer_id"),
        F.col("tier"),
        F.col("region"),
        F.coalesce(
            "ts", "event_ts", "event_time", "updated_at",
            "change_ts", "effective_ts", "created_at",
        ).alias("event_ts"),
        F.coalesce("seq", "seqno", "lsn").alias("seq"),
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_history")
def customers_history():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    df = (
        spark.read.table("customers_cdc_raw")
        .where(F.col("customer_id").isNotNull() & F.col("event_ts").isNotNull())
    )

    # Deterministic ordering: event_ts (ISO strings sort chronologically),
    # tie-broken by seq. This makes reprocessing reproduce the same history.
    w = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(
        F.col("event_ts").asc(), F.col("seq").asc_nulls_first()
    )

    prev_tier = F.lag("tier").over(w)
    prev_region = F.lag("region").over(w)

    # A new version begins whenever tier/region change (null-safe); the first
    # row per customer is naturally a change (prev is NULL).
    is_change = (
        (~F.col("tier").eqNullSafe(prev_tier))
        | (~F.col("region").eqNullSafe(prev_region))
    ).cast("int")

    wcum = w.rowsBetween(Window.unboundedPreceding, Window.currentRow)
    df = df.withColumn("grp", F.sum(is_change).over(wcum))

    # Collapse consecutive identical events into one version.
    grouped = df.groupBy("customer_id", "grp").agg(
        F.min("event_ts").alias("valid_from_str"),
        F.first("tier").alias("tier"),
        F.first("region").alias("region"),
    )

    # Non-overlapping periods: valid_to = next version's valid_from.
    w2 = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(F.col("valid_from_str").asc())
    next_from = F.lead("valid_from_str").over(w2)

    result = (
        grouped
        .withColumn("valid_from", F.to_timestamp("valid_from_str"))
        .withColumn("valid_to", F.to_timestamp(next_from))
        .withColumn("is_current", next_from.isNull())
    )

    return result.select(
        "customer_id", "tier", "region", "valid_from", "valid_to", "is_current"
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_current")
def customers_current():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    h = spark.read.table("customers_history")
    # Current = the customer's latest real change (open-ended period).
    return h.where(F.col("is_current")).select("customer_id", "tier", "region")

DATA = "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_customers_cdc_seed9001.ndjson"

# Explicit schema with many candidate field names so missing ones resolve to NULL
# (avoids UNRESOLVED_COLUMN) and unknown JSON fields are simply dropped.
SCHEMA = StructType([
    StructField("customer_id", StringType()),
    StructField("id", StringType()),
    StructField("cust_id", StringType()),
    StructField("tier", StringType()),
    StructField("region", StringType()),
    StructField("ts", StringType()),
    StructField("event_time", StringType()),
    StructField("event_ts", StringType()),
    StructField("updated_at", StringType()),
    StructField("changed_at", StringType()),
    StructField("change_ts", StringType()),
    StructField("timestamp", StringType()),
    StructField("valid_from", StringType()),
    StructField("seq", LongType()),
    StructField("sequence", LongType()),
    StructField("change_id", LongType()),
    StructField("lsn", LongType()),
    StructField("version", LongType()),
    StructField("op", StringType()),
])


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_cdc_raw")

    return SparkSession.active().read.schema(SCHEMA).json(DATA)


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_cdc_norm")
def customers_cdc_norm():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table("customers_cdc_raw")
    cid = F.coalesce(F.col("customer_id"), F.col("id"), F.col("cust_id"))
    ts = F.coalesce(
        F.to_timestamp("ts"),
        F.to_timestamp("event_time"),
        F.to_timestamp("event_ts"),
        F.to_timestamp("updated_at"),
        F.to_timestamp("changed_at"),
        F.to_timestamp("change_ts"),
        F.to_timestamp("timestamp"),
        F.to_timestamp("valid_from"),
    )
    seq = F.coalesce(
        F.col("seq"), F.col("sequence"), F.col("change_id"),
        F.col("lsn"), F.col("version"),
    )
    return df.select(
        cid.alias("customer_id"),
        F.col("tier").alias("tier"),
        F.col("region").alias("region"),
        ts.alias("event_ts"),
        seq.alias("seq"),
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_versions")
def customers_versions():
    df = (
        SparkSession.active().read.table("customers_cdc_norm")
        .where(F.col("customer_id").isNotNull() & F.col("event_ts").isNotNull())
    )

    # 1) Collapse contradictory records at the SAME instant to one deterministic winner.
    w_inst = Window.partitionBy("customer_id", "event_ts").orderBy(
        F.col("seq").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("tier").asc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("region").asc_nulls_last(),
    )
    ded = (
        df.withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(w_inst))
        .where(F.col("rn") == 1)
        .drop("rn")
    )

    # 2) Order per customer and detect real state changes (tier/region deltas).
    w_ord = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(
        F.col("event_ts").asc(), F.col("seq").asc_nulls_last()
    )
    with_prev = (
        ded.withColumn("prev_tier", F.lag("tier").over(w_ord))
        .withColumn("prev_region", F.lag("region").over(w_ord))
    )
    changed = (~F.col("tier").eqNullSafe(F.col("prev_tier"))) | (
        ~F.col("region").eqNullSafe(F.col("prev_region"))
    )
    starts = with_prev.where(changed)

    # 3) Build non-overlapping periods: valid_to = start of the next real change.
    w_v = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(F.col("event_ts").asc())
    versions = (
        starts.withColumn("valid_from", F.col("event_ts"))
        .withColumn("valid_to", F.lead("event_ts").over(w_v))
        .withColumn("is_current", F.col("valid_to").isNull())
    )
    return versions.select(
        "customer_id", "tier", "region", "valid_from", "valid_to", "is_current"
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_history")
def customers_history():
    return SparkSession.active().read.table("customers_versions").select(
        "customer_id", "tier", "region", "valid_from", "valid_to", "is_current"
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_current")
def customers_current():
    v = SparkSession.active().read.table("customers_versions")
    return v.where(F.col("is_current")).select("customer_id", "tier", "region")

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window
from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, StringType

DATA = "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_customers_cdc_seed11235.ndjson"

CAND_ID  = ["customer_id", "cust_id", "customerid", "id"]
CAND_TS  = ["ts", "event_ts", "event_time", "eventtime", "updated_at",
            "effective_ts", "effective_at", "effective_date", "changed_at",
            "change_ts", "created_at", "timestamp", "valid_from", "_ts"]
CAND_SEQ = ["seq", "seq_no", "seq_num", "sequence", "change_seq", "version",
            "op_seq", "lsn", "offset", "_seq", "change_id"]
EXTRA    = ["tier", "region", "op", "op_type", "operation"]

_ALLCOLS = sorted(set(CAND_ID + CAND_TS + CAND_SEQ + EXTRA))
SCHEMA = StructType([StructField(c, StringType(), True) for c in _ALLCOLS])


def _first(cols):
    # coalesce over candidate columns (all exist thanks to explicit schema)
    return F.coalesce(*[F.col(c) for c in cols])


def _parse_ts(ts_raw):
    secs   = ts_raw.rlike("^[0-9]{10}$")
    millis = ts_raw.rlike("^[0-9]{13}$")
    return (F.when(millis, (ts_raw.cast("long") / F.lit(1000)).cast("timestamp"))
             .when(secs, ts_raw.cast("long").cast("timestamp"))
             .otherwise(F.to_timestamp(ts_raw)))


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_history")
def customers_history():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    raw = spark.read.schema(SCHEMA).json(DATA)

    events = (raw
        .select(
            _first(CAND_ID).cast("string").alias("customer_id"),
            F.col("tier").cast("string").alias("tier"),
            F.col("region").cast("string").alias("region"),
            _parse_ts(_first(CAND_TS)).alias("ts"),
            _first(CAND_SEQ).cast("long").alias("seq"),
        )
        .where(F.col("customer_id").isNotNull() & F.col("ts").isNotNull()))

    order = [F.col("ts").asc(), F.col("seq").asc_nulls_last(),
             F.col("tier").asc_nulls_last(), F.col("region").asc_nulls_last()]
    w = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(*order)

    tagged = (events
        .withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(w))
        .withColumn("prev_tier", F.lag("tier").over(w))
        .withColumn("prev_region", F.lag("region").over(w)))

    # keep only rows that represent a real attribute change (or the first row)
    changes = tagged.where(
        (F.col("rn") == 1) |
        (~(F.col("tier").eqNullSafe(F.col("prev_tier")) &
           F.col("region").eqNullSafe(F.col("prev_region"))))
    )

    w2 = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(*order)
    hist = (changes
        .withColumn("valid_from", F.col("ts"))
        .withColumn("valid_to", F.lead("ts").over(w2))
        .withColumn("is_current", F.lead("ts").over(w2).isNull()))

    return hist.select("customer_id", "tier", "region",
                       "valid_from", "valid_to", "is_current")


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_current")
def customers_current():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    hist = spark.read.table("spark_catalog.default.customers_history")
    return (hist.where(F.col("is_current") == F.lit(True))
                .select("customer_id", "tier", "region"))

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window
from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, StringType

DATA = ("file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/"
        ".work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_customers_cdc_seed31415.ndjson")

# Superset schema: any field not present in a line parses to NULL (no unresolved-column error)
SCHEMA = StructType([
    StructField("customer_id", StringType()),
    StructField("id", StringType()),
    StructField("cust_id", StringType()),
    StructField("tier", StringType()),
    StructField("region", StringType()),
    StructField("ts", StringType()),
    StructField("event_ts", StringType()),
    StructField("event_time", StringType()),
    StructField("change_ts", StringType()),
    StructField("updated_at", StringType()),
    StructField("timestamp", StringType()),
    StructField("valid_from", StringType()),
    StructField("op", StringType()),
    StructField("seq", StringType()),
    StructField("sequence", StringType()),
    StructField("offset", StringType()),
])


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_versions")
def customers_versions():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    raw = spark.read.text(DATA)
    j = raw.select(F.from_json(F.col("value"), SCHEMA).alias("j")).select("j.*")

    cust = F.coalesce(F.col("customer_id"), F.col("id"), F.col("cust_id"))
    ts_str = F.coalesce(
        F.col("ts"), F.col("event_ts"), F.col("event_time"),
        F.col("change_ts"), F.col("updated_at"), F.col("timestamp"),
        F.col("valid_from"),
    )
    ts = F.coalesce(
        F.to_timestamp(ts_str),
        F.to_timestamp(F.from_unixtime(ts_str.cast("long"))),
    )
    seq_order = F.coalesce(
        F.col("seq").cast("long"), F.col("sequence").cast("long"),
        F.col("offset").cast("long"), F.lit(0).cast("long"),
    )

    df = (j
          .withColumn("customer_id", cust)
          .withColumn("event_ts", ts)
          .withColumn("seq_order", seq_order)
          .withColumn("tier", F.col("tier"))
          .withColumn("region", F.col("region"))
          .where(F.col("customer_id").isNotNull() & F.col("event_ts").isNotNull())
          .select("customer_id", "tier", "region", "event_ts", "seq_order"))

    # Deterministic ordering for reproducibility
    w = (Window.partitionBy("customer_id")
         .orderBy("event_ts", "seq_order", "tier", "region"))

    rn = F.row_number().over(w)
    prev_tier = F.lag("tier").over(w)
    prev_region = F.lag("region").over(w)

    # A "real change": first row for the customer, or (tier,region) differs from prior
    changed = (rn == 1) | ~(
        F.col("tier").eqNullSafe(prev_tier) & F.col("region").eqNullSafe(prev_region)
    )

    versions = df.withColumn("_changed", changed).where(F.col("_changed"))

    wv = (Window.partitionBy("customer_id")
          .orderBy("event_ts", "seq_order", "tier", "region"))

    out = (versions
           .withColumn("valid_from", F.col("event_ts"))
           .withColumn("valid_to", F.lead("event_ts").over(wv))
           .withColumn("is_current", F.lead("event_ts").over(wv).isNull())
           .select("customer_id", "tier", "region",
                   "valid_from", "valid_to", "is_current"))
    return out


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_history")
def customers_history():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    return (spark.read.table("spark_catalog.default.customers_versions")
            .select("customer_id", "tier", "region",
                    "valid_from", "valid_to", "is_current"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_current")
def customers_current():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    return (spark.read.table("spark_catalog.default.customers_versions")
            .where(F.col("is_current"))
            .select("customer_id", "tier", "region"))

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

PATH = "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_customers_cdc_seed16180.ndjson"


def _pick(cols, cands, default):
    low = {c.lower(): c for c in cols}
    for c in cands:
        if c in low:
            return low[c]
    return default


# Discover the real column names from the feed once, at import time,
# so we never reference a non-existent column (root cause of prior failures).
try:
    _cols = SparkSession.builder.getOrCreate().read.json(PATH).columns
except Exception:
    _cols = []

ID = _pick(_cols, ["customer_id", "customerid", "cust_id", "id"], "customer_id")
TIER = _pick(_cols, ["tier"], "tier")
REGION = _pick(_cols, ["region"], "region")
TS = _pick(_cols, ["ts", "event_time", "event_ts", "event_timestamp", "timestamp",
                   "updated_at", "change_ts", "change_time", "commit_ts", "op_ts",
                   "modified_at", "valid_from"], "ts")
SEQ = _pick(_cols, ["seq", "sequence", "seqnum", "seq_num", "offset", "lsn",
                    "version", "_seq"], None)


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_cdc_raw")
def customers_cdc_raw():
    return SparkSession.active().read.json(PATH)


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_history")
def customers_history():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table("customers_cdc_raw")
    seq_col = F.col(SEQ) if SEQ else F.lit(None)
    norm = df.select(
        F.col(ID).cast("string").alias("customer_id"),
        F.col(TIER).alias("tier"),
        F.col(REGION).alias("region"),
        F.col(TS).alias("valid_from"),
        seq_col.alias("_seq"),
    )

    # Deterministic per-customer ordering (stable across reprocessing).
    order = [F.col("valid_from").asc(), F.col("_seq").asc(),
             F.col("tier").asc(), F.col("region").asc()]
    w = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(*order)

    prev = (norm
            .withColumn("_ptier", F.lag("tier").over(w))
            .withColumn("_pregion", F.lag("region").over(w)))

    # Keep only real attribute changes -> removes contradictory/overlapping dups.
    changes = prev.where(
        F.col("_ptier").isNull()
        | (~F.col("tier").eqNullSafe(F.col("_ptier")))
        | (~F.col("region").eqNullSafe(F.col("_pregion")))
    )

    w2 = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(
        F.col("valid_from").asc(), F.col("_seq").asc())

    out = changes.withColumn("valid_to", F.lead("valid_from").over(w2))

    return out.select(
        "customer_id",
        "tier",
        "region",
        "valid_from",
        "valid_to",
        F.col("valid_to").isNull().alias("is_current"),
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_current")
def customers_current():
    h = SparkSession.active().read.table("customers_history")
    return h.where(F.col("is_current")).select("customer_id", "tier", "region")

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F
from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, StringType
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

DATA = "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_customers_cdc_seed14142.ndjson"

# Superset schema: any field absent in the feed simply materializes as NULL
# (no schema inference => no action inside the query function).
SCHEMA = StructType([
    StructField("customer_id", StringType()),
    StructField("id",          StringType()),
    StructField("tier",        StringType()),
    StructField("region",      StringType()),
    StructField("ts",          StringType()),
    StructField("event_ts",    StringType()),
    StructField("event_time",  StringType()),
    StructField("changed_at",  StringType()),
    StructField("updated_at",  StringType()),
    StructField("change_ts",   StringType()),
    StructField("timestamp",   StringType()),
    StructField("op",          StringType()),
    StructField("seq",         StringType()),
    StructField("sequence",    StringType()),
    StructField("version",     StringType()),
    StructField("offset",      StringType()),
])

CAT = "spark_catalog.default"

# Deterministic ordering: real change time first, then a numeric sequence
# tiebreak, then the attributes themselves for a fully stable total order.
def _order():
    return [
        F.col("event_ts").asc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("seq_num").asc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("tier").asc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("region").asc_nulls_last(),
    ]


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_raw")
def customers_raw():
    return SparkSession.active().read.schema(SCHEMA).json(DATA)


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_changes")
def customers_changes():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table(f"{CAT}.customers_raw")
    cid = F.coalesce("customer_id", "id")
    ts_raw = F.coalesce("ts", "event_ts", "event_time", "changed_at",
                        "updated_at", "change_ts", "timestamp")
    seq = F.coalesce("seq", "sequence", "version", "offset")
    return (
        df.select(
            cid.alias("customer_id"),
            F.col("tier").alias("tier"),
            F.col("region").alias("region"),
            F.to_timestamp(ts_raw).alias("event_ts"),
            seq.cast("double").alias("seq_num"),
        )
        .where(F.col("customer_id").isNotNull())
        .where(F.col("tier").isNotNull() | F.col("region").isNotNull())
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_history")
def customers_history():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table(f"{CAT}.customers_changes")
    w = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(*_order())

    # Collapse consecutive identical (tier, region) events so that only
    # *real* attribute changes create a new version.
    marked = (
        df.withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(w))
          .withColumn("prev_tier", F.lag("tier").over(w))
          .withColumn("prev_region", F.lag("region").over(w))
    )
    kept = marked.where(
        (F.col("rn") == 1)
        | ~(F.col("tier").eqNullSafe(F.col("prev_tier"))
            & F.col("region").eqNullSafe(F.col("prev_region")))
    )

    # Contiguous, non-overlapping periods: [valid_from, next valid_from).
    w2 = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(*_order())
    nxt = F.lead("event_ts").over(w2)
    return (
        kept.withColumn("valid_from", F.col("event_ts"))
            .withColumn("valid_to", nxt)
            .withColumn("is_current", nxt.isNull())
            .select("customer_id", "tier", "region",
                    "valid_from", "valid_to", "is_current")
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="customers_current")
def customers_current():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table(f"{CAT}.customers_history")
    return (
        df.where(F.col("is_current"))
          .select("customer_id", "tier", "region")
    )

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F

DATA = "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed42.ndjson"


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_raw")
def orders_raw():
    # Read each NDJSON line as raw text so mixed/evolving shapes never break inference.
    return SparkSession.active().read.text(DATA)


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_parsed")
def orders_parsed():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table("orders_raw")
    v = F.col("value")

    # --- timestamp: tolerant, no strict parsing (avoids CANNOT_PARSE_TIMESTAMP) ---
    ts = F.regexp_replace(
        F.coalesce(
            F.get_json_object(v, "$.timestamp"),
            F.get_json_object(v, "$.event_time"),
            F.get_json_object(v, "$.ts"),
            F.get_json_object(v, "$.created_at"),
            F.get_json_object(v, "$.date"),
            F.get_json_object(v, "$.order_date"),
            F.lit(""),
        ),
        '"',
        "",
    )

    # epoch (secs or millis) -> UTC day via pure integer arithmetic (no session tz)
    epoch = F.when(ts.rlike(r"^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$"), ts.cast("double"))
    epoch_secs = F.when(epoch >= F.lit(1e12), epoch / F.lit(1000.0)).otherwise(epoch)
    epoch_day = F.floor(epoch_secs / F.lit(86400.0)).cast("int")
    date_epoch = F.date_add(F.to_date(F.lit("1970-01-01")), epoch_day)

    # ISO / "yyyy-MM-dd ..." -> take the calendar-day prefix (UTC as emitted)
    date_str = F.when(
        ts.rlike(r"^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}"),
        F.to_date(F.substring(ts, 1, 10)),
    )

    # sensible default for genuinely missing/unparseable dates (keeps revenue complete)
    event_date = F.coalesce(date_str, date_epoch, F.to_date(F.lit("1970-01-01")))

    # --- amount: handle scalar (old) and line-item list (new) shapes ---
    amt = F.get_json_object(v, "$.amount")
    items = F.from_json(
        amt,
        "array<struct<amount:double,price:double,qty:double,quantity:double,"
        "line_total:double,total:double,value:double>>",
    )
    item_sum = F.aggregate(
        items,
        F.lit(0.0),
        lambda acc, x: acc
        + F.coalesce(
            x["line_total"],
            x["total"],
            x["amount"],
            x["value"],
            x["price"] * F.coalesce(x["qty"], x["quantity"], F.lit(1.0)),
            F.lit(0.0),
        ),
    )

    # list -> item_sum ; scalar number -> cast ; missing -> 0.0
    revenue = F.coalesce(item_sum, amt.cast("double"), F.lit(0.0))

    return df.select(
        event_date.alias("event_date"),
        revenue.cast("double").alias("rev"),
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table("orders_parsed")
    return (
        df.groupBy("event_date")
        .agg(F.sum("rev").alias("revenue"))
        .select("event_date", "revenue")
    )

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F

DATA = ("file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/"
        ".work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed1337.ndjson")

# Schema used to interpret the NEW payload shape where `amount` is a list of
# line items. We keep it permissive: any of these fields may be present.
_ITEM_SCHEMA = ("array<struct<amount:double,price:double,unit_price:double,"
                "qty:double,quantity:double,total:double,subtotal:double,"
                "line_total:double>>")


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_raw")
def orders_raw():
    # Read tolerantly as raw text so a schema conflict on `amount`
    # (scalar in old records vs. list in new records) never rejects a row.
    return SparkSession.active().read.text(DATA)


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_typed")
def orders_typed():
    line = F.col("value")

    # --- date field: try every plausible name, tolerate missing ---
    raw_date = F.coalesce(
        F.get_json_object(line, "$.event_date"),
        F.get_json_object(line, "$.event_ts"),
        F.get_json_object(line, "$.order_ts"),
        F.get_json_object(line, "$.timestamp"),
        F.get_json_object(line, "$.ts"),
        F.get_json_object(line, "$.created_at"),
        F.get_json_object(line, "$.order_date"),
        F.get_json_object(line, "$.date"),
    )

    is_epoch = raw_date.rlike("^[0-9]+$")
    # millis (>=13 digits) vs seconds
    epoch_sec = F.when(F.length(raw_date) > F.lit(12),
                       (raw_date.cast("long") / F.lit(1000))
                       ).otherwise(raw_date.cast("long"))
    epoch_day = F.floor(epoch_sec / F.lit(86400)).cast("int")
    # Timezone-independent UTC calendar day (no session.timeZone mutation).
    date_from_epoch = F.date_add(F.to_date(F.lit("1970-01-01")), epoch_day)

    # ISO / date-prefixed strings are already UTC-anchored (…Z / plain date):
    # take the leading yyyy-MM-dd.
    date_from_iso = F.coalesce(
        F.to_date(F.substring(raw_date, 1, 10), "yyyy-MM-dd"),
        F.to_date(F.substring(raw_date, 1, 10), "yyyy/MM/dd"),
    )

    event_date = F.when(is_epoch, date_from_epoch).otherwise(date_from_iso)

    # --- amount field: scalar OR list of line items, or missing ---
    amount_raw = F.trim(F.get_json_object(line, "$.amount"))
    is_list = amount_raw.startswith("[")

    # list of plain numbers -> sum
    arr_num = F.from_json(amount_raw, "array<double>")
    num_sum = F.aggregate(
        F.coalesce(arr_num, F.array()),
        F.lit(0.0),
        lambda acc, x: acc + F.coalesce(x, F.lit(0.0)),
    )

    # list of line-item objects -> per-item value then sum
    arr_obj = F.from_json(amount_raw, _ITEM_SCHEMA)
    item_vals = F.transform(
        F.coalesce(arr_obj, F.array()),
        lambda it: F.coalesce(
            it["total"],
            it["line_total"],
            it["subtotal"],
            it["amount"],
            F.coalesce(it["price"], it["unit_price"]) *
            F.coalesce(it["qty"], it["quantity"], F.lit(1.0)),
            it["price"],
            it["unit_price"],
            F.lit(0.0),
        ),
    )
    obj_sum = F.aggregate(item_vals, F.lit(0.0), lambda acc, x: acc + x)

    list_revenue = F.when(obj_sum != F.lit(0.0), obj_sum).otherwise(num_sum)

    revenue = (F.when(amount_raw.isNull(), F.lit(0.0))          # missing -> default 0
                .when(is_list, list_revenue)                    # new shape
                .otherwise(amount_raw.cast("double")))          # old scalar shape
    revenue = F.coalesce(revenue, F.lit(0.0))

    return (SparkSession.active().read.table("orders_raw")
            .select(event_date.alias("event_date"),
                    revenue.cast("double").alias("revenue")))


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    src = SparkSession.active().read.table("orders_typed")
    return (src.groupBy("event_date")
               .agg(F.sum("revenue").cast("double").alias("revenue"))
               .select(F.col("event_date").cast("date").alias("event_date"),
                       "revenue"))

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F

DATA = "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed2718.ndjson"

TS_PATHS = [
    "$.event_time", "$.event_ts", "$.event_timestamp", "$.timestamp",
    "$.ts", "$.order_time", "$.order_ts", "$.created_at", "$.created",
    "$.date", "$.event_date", "$.order_date", "$.time",
]

ELEM = ("array<struct<amount:double,price:double,qty:double,quantity:double,"
        "unit_price:double,line_total:double,total:double,value:double>>")


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_raw")
def orders_raw():
    # Read as raw text: full control over tolerant JSON parsing, no schema clash.
    return SparkSession.active().read.text(DATA)


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    df = spark.read.table("orders_raw")

    line = F.col("value")
    amt_raw = F.get_json_object(line, "$.amount")
    ts_raw = F.coalesce(*[F.get_json_object(line, p) for p in TS_PATHS])

    df = df.withColumn("amt_raw", amt_raw).withColumn("ts_raw", ts_raw)

    # --- amount shape 1: scalar number (null if it's actually an array) ---
    scalar_val = F.expr("try_cast(amt_raw as double)")

    # --- amount shape 2: list of plain numbers ---
    arr_d = F.from_json(F.col("amt_raw"), "array<double>")
    arr_d_sum = F.aggregate(
        arr_d, F.lit(0.0),
        lambda acc, x: acc + F.coalesce(x, F.lit(0.0)),
    )

    # --- amount shape 3: list of line-item objects ---
    arr_s = F.from_json(F.col("amt_raw"), ELEM)

    def item_val(x):
        return F.coalesce(
            x["line_total"],
            x["total"],
            x["amount"],
            x["value"],
            x["unit_price"] * F.coalesce(x["qty"], x["quantity"], F.lit(1.0)),
            x["price"] * F.coalesce(x["qty"], x["quantity"], F.lit(1.0)),
            x["price"],
            F.lit(0.0),
        )

    arr_s_sum = F.aggregate(
        arr_s, F.lit(0.0),
        lambda acc, x: acc + item_val(x),
    )

    # Only one representation is non-null per row; missing amount -> 0.
    row_rev = (
        F.coalesce(scalar_val, F.lit(0.0))
        + F.coalesce(arr_d_sum, F.lit(0.0))
        + F.coalesce(arr_s_sum, F.lit(0.0))
    )

    # --- deterministic UTC calendar day, no session-tz / conf dependency ---
    ts_parsed = F.to_timestamp(F.col("ts_raw"))          # handles ISO incl. 'Z'
    epoch_num = F.expr("try_cast(ts_raw as double)")     # raw epoch fallback
    epoch_norm = F.when(epoch_num > F.lit(1e12), epoch_num / F.lit(1000.0)) \
                  .otherwise(epoch_num)                   # ms -> s if needed
    epoch = F.coalesce(F.unix_timestamp(ts_parsed).cast("double"), epoch_norm)

    df = (
        df.withColumn("row_rev", row_rev)
          .withColumn("epoch", epoch)
          .withColumn("day_num", F.floor(F.col("epoch") / F.lit(86400.0)))
          .withColumn(
              "event_date",
              F.expr("date_add(date'1970-01-01', cast(day_num as int))"),
          )
    )

    return (
        df.groupBy("event_date")
          .agg(F.sum(F.coalesce(F.col("row_rev"), F.lit(0.0))).alias("revenue"))
    )

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F

DATA_PATH = "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed3141.ndjson"


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_raw")
def orders_raw():
    # One row per NDJSON line; keeps parsing fully under our control so both
    # old (scalar amount) and new (list-of-line-items amount) shapes survive.
    return SparkSession.active().read.text(DATA_PATH)


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table("orders_raw")
    v = F.col("value")

    # ---- date extraction (tolerant to field name + format) ----
    date_str = F.coalesce(
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.event_date"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.order_date"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.date"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.timestamp"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.ts"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.created_at"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.time"),
    )
    # UTC calendar day: for ISO strings the leading 10 chars are the UTC date
    # (feeds emit Z/UTC); fall back to timestamp parse, then epoch seconds.
    event_date = F.coalesce(
        F.to_date(F.substring(date_str, 1, 10)),
        F.to_date(F.to_timestamp(date_str)),
        F.to_date(F.from_unixtime(date_str.cast("long"))),
    )

    # ---- amount extraction (scalar OR list of line items OR missing) ----
    amt_str = F.get_json_object(v, "$.amount")

    scalar_amt = amt_str.cast("double")

    # list of plain numbers, e.g. [10.0, 5.0]
    num_arr = F.from_json(amt_str, "array<double>")
    num_sum = F.aggregate(
        num_arr, F.lit(0.0),
        lambda acc, x: acc + F.coalesce(x, F.lit(0.0)),
    )

    # list of line-item objects with varied field names
    li_schema = ("array<struct<amount:double,total:double,line_total:double,"
                 "price:double,unit_price:double,qty:double,quantity:double>>")
    li_arr = F.from_json(amt_str, li_schema)
    li_sum = F.aggregate(
        li_arr, F.lit(0.0),
        lambda acc, x: acc + F.coalesce(
            x["amount"], x["total"], x["line_total"],
            x["unit_price"] * F.coalesce(x["qty"], x["quantity"], F.lit(1.0)),
            x["price"] * F.coalesce(x["qty"], x["quantity"], F.lit(1.0)),
            x["price"], F.lit(0.0),
        ),
    )

    # For a list, exactly one interpretation carries the value; take the larger.
    list_amt = F.greatest(F.coalesce(num_sum, F.lit(0.0)),
                          F.coalesce(li_sum, F.lit(0.0)))

    amount = (
        F.when(amt_str.isNull(), F.lit(0.0))                    # missing -> default 0
         .when(amt_str.startswith("["), list_amt)              # new list shape
         .otherwise(F.coalesce(scalar_amt, F.lit(0.0)))        # old scalar shape
    )

    parsed = df.select(event_date.alias("event_date"), amount.alias("amount"))

    return (
        parsed.groupBy("event_date")
              .agg(F.sum("amount").alias("revenue"))
    )

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F

DATA = "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed5772.ndjson"

CAT = "spark_catalog.default"

# amount as a plain scalar
NUM = "struct<amount:double>"
# amount as a list of plain numbers
ARR_N = "struct<amount:array<double>>"
# amount as a list of line-item objects (tolerant to field naming)
ARR_O = ("struct<amount:array<struct<amount:double,price:double,qty:double,"
         "quantity:double,value:double,total:double,subtotal:double,line_total:double>>>")
# candidate timestamp/date carrying fields (kept as strings; parsed null-safely)
META = ("struct<ts:string,timestamp:string,event_time:string,created_at:string,"
        "event_date:string,date:string,order_date:string,time:string>")


@dp.materialized_view(name="bronze_orders")
def bronze_orders():
    # raw NDJSON lines -> avoids JSON schema-inference conflicts on the
    # scalar-vs-list `amount` field.
    return SparkSession.active().read.text(DATA)


@dp.materialized_view(name="parsed_orders")
def parsed_orders():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table(f"{CAT}.bronze_orders")

    num = F.from_json("value", NUM).getField("amount")
    arr_n = F.from_json("value", ARR_N).getField("amount")
    arr_o = F.from_json("value", ARR_O).getField("amount")
    meta = F.from_json("value", META)

    # sum of a numeric line-item list (null array -> null -> 0)
    sum_n = F.coalesce(
        F.aggregate(arr_n, F.lit(0.0), lambda a, x: a + F.coalesce(x, F.lit(0.0))),
        F.lit(0.0),
    )

    def item_val(x):
        return F.coalesce(
            x.getField("total"),
            x.getField("line_total"),
            x.getField("subtotal"),
            x.getField("value"),
            x.getField("amount"),
            x.getField("price")
            * F.coalesce(x.getField("qty"), x.getField("quantity"), F.lit(1.0)),
            x.getField("price"),
            F.lit(0.0),
        )

    sum_o = F.coalesce(
        F.aggregate(arr_o, F.lit(0.0), lambda a, x: a + item_val(x)),
        F.lit(0.0),
    )

    # complete revenue across both shapes; missing amount -> 0 (sensible default)
    record_amount = F.coalesce(num, F.lit(0.0)) + sum_n + sum_o

    raw_ts = F.coalesce(
        meta.getField("ts"),
        meta.getField("timestamp"),
        meta.getField("event_time"),
        meta.getField("created_at"),
        meta.getField("event_date"),
        meta.getField("date"),
        meta.getField("order_date"),
        meta.getField("time"),
    )

    df = df.withColumn("record_amount", record_amount)
    df = df.withColumn("raw_ts", raw_ts)

    # ANSI-safe numeric epoch detection (no throwing cast)
    df = df.withColumn("epoch", F.expr("try_cast(raw_ts as double)"))
    df = df.withColumn(
        "epoch_sec",
        F.when(F.col("epoch") > F.lit(1e14), F.col("epoch") / F.lit(1e6))
        .when(F.col("epoch") > F.lit(1e11), F.col("epoch") / F.lit(1e3))
        .otherwise(F.col("epoch")),
    )

    # null-safe timestamp parsing (no throws under ANSI / EXCEPTION time policy)
    ts_iso = F.try_to_timestamp(F.col("raw_ts"))
    ts_dateonly = F.try_to_timestamp(F.col("raw_ts"), F.lit("yyyy-MM-dd"))

    # seconds since epoch (UTC instant) -> UTC calendar day, tz-independent
    sec = F.coalesce(
        F.when(F.col("epoch").isNotNull(), F.col("epoch_sec")),
        F.unix_timestamp(ts_iso).cast("double"),
        F.unix_timestamp(ts_dateonly).cast("double"),
    )
    event_date = F.date_from_unix_date(F.floor(sec / F.lit(86400.0)).cast("int"))

    return df.withColumn("event_date", event_date).select("event_date", "record_amount")


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table(f"{CAT}.parsed_orders")
    return (
        df.groupBy("event_date")
        .agg(F.sum("record_amount").alias("revenue"))
        .select(F.col("event_date").cast("date").alias("event_date"), "revenue")
    )

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, types as T

DATA = "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed8675.ndjson"


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_raw")
def orders_raw():
    # Read line-by-line as raw text so JSON schema inference does not choke on
    # the amount field arriving as EITHER a scalar number OR an array of items.
    return SparkSession.active().read.text(DATA)


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table("orders_raw")
    v = F.col("value")

    # ---- timestamp -> UTC calendar day (tolerant of field name / format) ----
    ts_raw = F.coalesce(
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.event_time"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.event_ts"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.ts"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.timestamp"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.created_at"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.order_ts"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.order_time"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.order_date"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.date"),
    )
    ts_trim = F.trim(ts_raw)
    is_epoch = ts_trim.rlike("^[0-9]+$")
    # epoch millis (>=13 digits) -> seconds; else already seconds
    epoch_secs = F.when(F.length(ts_trim) >= F.lit(13),
                        ts_trim.cast("double") / F.lit(1000.0)) \
                  .otherwise(ts_trim.cast("double"))
    event_date = F.when(
        is_epoch,
        F.date_from_unix_date(F.floor(epoch_secs / F.lit(86400.0)).cast("int"))  # tz-free UTC day
    ).otherwise(
        F.to_date(F.substring(ts_trim, 1, 10))  # "YYYY-MM-DD..." UTC/naive date part
    )

    # ---- amount: scalar number OR array of line items ----
    amt_raw = F.get_json_object(v, "$.amount")
    amt_trim = F.trim(amt_raw)
    is_arr = amt_trim.startswith("[")

    nums = F.from_json(amt_raw, T.ArrayType(T.DoubleType()))
    objs = F.from_json(amt_raw, T.ArrayType(T.StructType([
        T.StructField("amount", T.DoubleType()),
        T.StructField("price", T.DoubleType()),
        T.StructField("qty", T.DoubleType()),
        T.StructField("quantity", T.DoubleType()),
        T.StructField("total", T.DoubleType()),
        T.StructField("unit_price", T.DoubleType()),
        T.StructField("line_total", T.DoubleType()),
        T.StructField("subtotal", T.DoubleType()),
    ])))

    # array-of-numbers path (objects parse to null elements here -> contribute 0)
    sum_nums = F.coalesce(
        F.aggregate(nums, F.lit(0.0), lambda acc, x: acc + F.coalesce(x, F.lit(0.0))),
        F.lit(0.0),
    )
    # array-of-objects path (bare numbers parse to null struct here -> contribute 0)
    sum_objs = F.coalesce(
        F.aggregate(
            objs, F.lit(0.0),
            lambda acc, x: acc + F.coalesce(
                x["line_total"], x["subtotal"], x["total"], x["amount"],
                x["price"] * F.coalesce(x["qty"], x["quantity"], F.lit(1.0)),
                x["unit_price"] * F.coalesce(x["qty"], x["quantity"], F.lit(1.0)),
                x["price"], x["unit_price"], F.lit(0.0),
            ),
        ),
        F.lit(0.0),
    )

    array_amt = sum_nums + sum_objs
    scalar_amt = amt_trim.cast("double")
    amount = F.coalesce(F.when(is_arr, array_amt).otherwise(scalar_amt), F.lit(0.0))

    parsed = df.select(
        event_date.alias("event_date"),
        amount.cast("double").alias("amount"),
    ).where(F.col("event_date").isNotNull())

    return (
        parsed.groupBy("event_date")
        .agg(F.sum("amount").alias("revenue"))
        .select(F.col("event_date").cast("date").alias("event_date"), "revenue")
    )

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F

DATA = "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed11235.ndjson"


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_raw")
def orders_raw():
    # Read tolerantly as raw text so mixed field shapes never break ingestion.
    return SparkSession.active().read.text(DATA)


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_normalized")
def orders_normalized():
    v = F.col("value")

    # --- amount: may be a scalar number OR a list of line items OR missing ---
    scalar_amt = F.expr("try_cast(get_json_object(value, '$.amount') as double)")

    struct_rev = F.expr(
        "aggregate("
        "  from_json(get_json_object(value,'$.amount'),"
        "    'array<struct<amount:double,price:double,qty:double,quantity:double,"
        "line_total:double,total:double,unit_price:double,subtotal:double>>'),"
        "  0D,"
        "  (acc, x) -> acc + coalesce(x.line_total, x.subtotal, x.total, x.amount,"
        "     x.price * coalesce(x.quantity, x.qty, 1D),"
        "     x.unit_price * coalesce(x.quantity, x.qty, 1D), 0D))"
    )

    double_rev = F.expr(
        "aggregate(from_json(get_json_object(value,'$.amount'),'array<double>'),"
        " 0D, (acc, x) -> acc + x)"
    )

    # scalar wins; else sum of structured line items; else numeric array; else 0
    amount = F.coalesce(scalar_amt, struct_rev, double_rev, F.lit(0.0))

    # --- event timestamp: tolerate many field names / formats ---
    ts_str = F.coalesce(
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.event_timestamp"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.event_time"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.event_date"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.timestamp"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.ts"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.created_at"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.order_date"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.order_ts"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.date"),
        F.get_json_object(v, "$.time"),
    )

    # ISO-ish date: pull the yyyy-MM-dd prefix out of a date/timestamp string
    date_token = F.regexp_extract(ts_str, r"(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2})", 1)
    date_token = F.when(date_token != "", date_token)
    date_iso = F.to_date(date_token, "yyyy-MM-dd")

    # epoch fallback (seconds or millis) -> UTC calendar day
    epoch_long = F.when(ts_str.rlike(r"^\d{9,}$"), ts_str.cast("long"))
    sec = F.when(
        epoch_long > F.lit(100000000000), (epoch_long / F.lit(1000)).cast("long")
    ).otherwise(epoch_long)
    date_epoch = F.to_date(F.timestamp_seconds(sec))

    event_date = F.coalesce(date_iso, date_epoch)

    df = SparkSession.active().read.table("orders_raw")
    return df.select(
        event_date.alias("event_date"),
        amount.cast("double").alias("amount"),
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table("orders_normalized")
    return (
        df.where(F.col("event_date").isNotNull())
        .groupBy(F.col("event_date").cast("date").alias("event_date"))
        .agg(F.sum("amount").alias("revenue"))
    )

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F

DATA_PATH = "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed27182.ndjson"

TS_KEYS = ["ts", "timestamp", "event_time", "event_ts", "created_at",
           "order_ts", "order_date", "date", "time"]


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_parsed")
def orders_parsed():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    raw = spark.read.text(DATA_PATH)
    v = F.col("value")

    # tolerant field extraction — never assume a fixed shape
    ts_str = F.coalesce(*[F.get_json_object(v, f"$.{k}") for k in TS_KEYS])
    amt_raw = F.get_json_object(v, "$.amount")
    base = raw.select(ts_str.alias("ts_str"), amt_raw.alias("amt_raw"))

    ts = F.col("ts_str")

    # --- event_date: derive the UTC calendar day, never throwing on a bad format ---
    ts_parsed = F.coalesce(
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(ts_str)"),                          # ISO incl. 'T'/offset
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(ts_str, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')"),
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(ts_str, 'MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss')"),
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(ts_str, 'MM/dd/yyyy')"),
        F.expr("try_to_timestamp(ts_str, 'yyyy/MM/dd')"),
    )
    # cast(timestamp -> long) is the UTC instant; //86400 gives the UTC day (tz-independent)
    days_ts = (ts_parsed.cast("long") / F.lit(86400.0)).cast("int")
    date_from_ts = F.date_add(F.to_date(F.lit("1970-01-01")), days_ts)

    # ISO date prefix fallback
    date_from_str = F.when(ts.rlike(r"^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}"),
                           F.to_date(F.substring(ts, 1, 10)))

    # raw epoch (seconds or millis) fallback
    num = F.when(ts.rlike(r"^\d+(\.\d+)?$"), ts.cast("double"))
    epoch_sec = F.when(num > F.lit(1e12), num / F.lit(1000.0)).otherwise(num)
    days_ep = (epoch_sec / F.lit(86400.0)).cast("int")
    date_from_epoch = F.date_add(F.to_date(F.lit("1970-01-01")), days_ep)

    event_date = F.coalesce(date_from_ts, date_from_str, date_from_epoch,
                            F.to_date(F.lit("1970-01-01")))

    # --- amount: scalar OR numeric array OR list-of-line-item objects ---
    a = F.col("amt_raw")
    is_arr = F.coalesce(a.rlike(r"^\s*\["), F.lit(False))
    is_obj = F.coalesce(a.rlike(r"\{"), F.lit(False))

    scalar_amt = F.when(~is_arr, F.expr("try_cast(amt_raw as double)"))

    num_arr = F.when(
        is_arr & ~is_obj,
        F.expr("aggregate(from_json(amt_raw,'array<double>'), "
               "cast(0.0 as double), (acc,x)->acc+coalesce(x, cast(0.0 as double)))"),
    )

    obj_arr = F.when(
        is_obj,
        F.expr(
            "aggregate("
            "transform(from_json(amt_raw,'array<struct<amount:double,price:double,"
            "qty:double,quantity:double,line_total:double,subtotal:double,total:double,"
            "value:double,unit_price:double>>'), "
            "x -> coalesce(x.amount, x.total, x.line_total, x.subtotal, x.value, "
            "coalesce(x.price,x.unit_price)*coalesce(x.qty,x.quantity,cast(1.0 as double)), "
            "coalesce(x.price,x.unit_price), cast(0.0 as double))), "
            "cast(0.0 as double), (acc,x)->acc+x)"
        ),
    )

    amount = F.coalesce(scalar_amt, num_arr, obj_arr, F.lit(0.0)).cast("double")

    return base.select(event_date.alias("event_date"), amount.alias("amount"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    src = SparkSession.active().read.table("orders_parsed")
    return (src.groupBy("event_date")
               .agg(F.sum("amount").alias("revenue")))

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, types as T

PATH = "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed14142.ndjson"

# candidate field names for the order timestamp (old vs new shapes)
TS_FIELDS = [
    "ts", "timestamp", "time", "event_time", "event_ts",
    "order_ts", "order_time", "created_at", "created",
    "date", "event_date", "order_date",
]

# line-item schema is unioned across plausible key names so array-shaped
# amounts sum correctly regardless of the exact field the upstream emits.
ITEM_SCHEMA = T.ArrayType(T.StructType([
    T.StructField("amount", T.DoubleType()),
    T.StructField("price", T.DoubleType()),
    T.StructField("qty", T.DoubleType()),
    T.StructField("quantity", T.DoubleType()),
    T.StructField("total", T.DoubleType()),
]))


@dp.materialized_view(name="bronze_orders")
def bronze_orders():
    # Read raw lines; do NOT let JSON inference choke on the amount union type.
    return SparkSession.active().read.text(PATH)


@dp.materialized_view(name="gold_daily")
def gold_daily():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table("bronze_orders")

    v = F.col("value")

    # --- tolerant amount extraction (scalar number OR list of line items) ---
    amount_raw = F.get_json_object(v, "$.amount")
    is_list = F.coalesce(amount_raw.startswith("["), F.lit(False))

    scalar_amt = F.when(~is_list, amount_raw.cast("double"))

    items = F.from_json(amount_raw, ITEM_SCHEMA)
    per_item = lambda acc, x: acc + F.coalesce(
        x["amount"],
        x["total"],
        x["price"] * F.coalesce(x["qty"], x["quantity"], F.lit(1.0)),
        x["price"],
        F.lit(0.0),
    )
    list_amt = F.aggregate(items, F.lit(0.0), per_item)

    # missing amount -> sensible default 0.0 so the record still loads
    revenue_per_order = F.coalesce(scalar_amt, list_amt, F.lit(0.0))

    # --- tolerant timestamp extraction across both record shapes ---
    ts_str = F.coalesce(*[F.get_json_object(v, f"$.{f}") for f in TS_FIELDS])

    is_num = F.coalesce(ts_str.rlike(r"^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$"), F.lit(False))
    epoch = ts_str.cast("double")
    # normalise millis -> seconds
    epoch_sec = F.when(epoch > F.lit(1e12), epoch / 1000.0).otherwise(epoch)
    # UTC day count is tz-independent: floor(epoch_sec / 86400)
    day_from_epoch = F.date_add(F.to_date(F.lit("1970-01-01")),
                                (epoch_sec / 86400.0).cast("int"))
    # ISO/date strings: the leading yyyy-MM-dd is already the UTC calendar day
    day_from_str = F.to_date(F.substring(ts_str, 1, 10))

    event_date = F.when(is_num, day_from_epoch).otherwise(day_from_str)

    parsed = df.select(
        event_date.alias("event_date"),
        revenue_per_order.alias("revenue_per_order"),
    )

    return (
        parsed
        .groupBy("event_date")
        .agg(F.sum("revenue_per_order").alias("revenue"))
        .select(F.col("event_date").cast("date").alias("event_date"), "revenue")
    )

#!/usr/bin/env python
"""
p12_quarantine_dlq -- imperative PySpark transform.

Reads messy NDJSON orders, splits into:
  * gold_daily      : per-order daily revenue rollup (UTC calendar day)
  * clean_orders    : deduplicated by order_id (amount, category)
  * rejected (DLQ)  : every unprocessable record retained WITH a reason
Guarantees: kept + rejected reconcile exactly to the source record count.
"""

import os
import sys

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
from pyspark.sql import functions as F
from pyspark.sql.window import Window


def get_input_path():
    p = os.environ.get("AGENT_INPUT_PATH")
    if p:
        return p
    # fall back to the seed dataset shipped with the task
    return ("/home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/"
            ".work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed1337.ndjson")


def pick(cols_lower, candidates):
    for c in candidates:
        if c in cols_lower:
            return cols_lower[c]
    return None


def main():
    spark = (
        SparkSession.builder
        .appName("p12_quarantine_dlq")
        .getOrCreate()
    )
    # Deterministic UTC calendar day; session-local, we own this session.
    spark.conf.set("spark.sql.session.timeZone", "UTC")

    input_path = get_input_path()

    out_gold = os.environ.get(
        "AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH",
        os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "gold_daily.parquet"),
    )
    out_secondary = os.environ.get(
        "AGENT_DEDUP_PATH",
        os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "secondary_output.parquet"),
    )
    # third contract table (DLQ) -- keep alongside the others
    out_rejected = os.path.join(
        os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(out_gold)), "rejected.parquet"
    )

    # ---- read (PERMISSIVE: never drop a line; corrupt lines are retained) ----
    raw = (
        spark.read
        .option("mode", "PERMISSIVE")
        .option("columnNameOfCorruptRecord", "_corrupt_record")
        .json(input_path)
    )
    if "_corrupt_record" not in raw.columns:
        raw = raw.withColumn("_corrupt_record", F.lit(None).cast("string"))

    raw = raw.persist()
    total = raw.count()  # SOURCE record count (one row per input line)

    cols_lower = {c.lower(): c for c in raw.columns if c != "_corrupt_record"}
    id_col = pick(cols_lower, ["order_id", "orderid", "id", "order", "oid"])
    amt_col = pick(cols_lower, ["amount", "revenue", "price", "total", "value", "amt"])
    cat_col = pick(cols_lower, ["category", "cat", "type", "segment", "kind"])
    ts_col = pick(cols_lower, ["event_time", "event_timestamp", "event_date",
                               "timestamp", "ts", "created_at", "order_ts",
                               "time", "datetime", "order_date", "date"])

    def col_or_null(name):
