from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, types as T

PATH = ("file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/"
        ".work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_payments_seed31415.ndjson")

# Explicit superset schema: every plausible field is declared (as string) so the
# plan resolves regardless of which names the messy upstream actually emits.
_FIELDS = [
    "type", "kind", "record_type", "event_type", "source", "feed",
    "payment_id", "id", "event_id", "settlement_id", "txn_id",
    "amount", "value", "settled_amount", "settled_value",
    "currency", "ccy", "currency_code", "cur",
    "rate", "fx_rate", "usd_rate", "rate_to_usd", "usd_per_unit",
    "rate_usd", "conversion_rate",
    "settlement_date", "date", "day", "event_date", "as_of", "effective_date",
    "ts", "timestamp", "event_time", "event_ts", "settled_at",
    "status", "settled", "state",
    "usd_total", "total_usd", "expected_usd", "bank_total", "total",
    "amount_usd", "usd_amount",
]
SCHEMA = T.StructType([T.StructField(n, T.StringType(), True) for n in _FIELDS])


@dp.materialized_view(name="settlement_bronze")
def settlement_bronze():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    df = spark.read.schema(SCHEMA).json(PATH)

    rec_type = F.lower(F.coalesce(
        F.col("type"), F.col("kind"), F.col("record_type"),
        F.col("event_type"), F.lit("")))
    src = F.lower(F.coalesce(F.col("source"), F.col("feed"), F.lit("")))
    ccy = F.upper(F.coalesce(
        F.col("currency"), F.col("ccy"), F.col("currency_code"), F.col("cur")))
    amt = F.coalesce(*[F.col(c).cast("double") for c in
                       ["amount", "value", "settled_amount", "settled_value"]])
    rate = F.coalesce(*[F.col(c).cast("double") for c in
                        ["rate", "fx_rate", "usd_rate", "rate_to_usd",
                         "usd_per_unit", "rate_usd", "conversion_rate"]])
    # UTC calendar day derived from an explicit date field or a timestamp prefix.
    sdate = F.to_date(F.coalesce(
        F.col("settlement_date"), F.col("date"), F.col("day"),
        F.col("event_date"), F.col("as_of"), F.col("effective_date"),
        F.col("ts"), F.col("timestamp"), F.col("event_time"),
        F.col("event_ts"), F.col("settled_at")))
    bank_usd = F.coalesce(*[F.col(c).cast("double") for c in
                            ["usd_total", "total_usd", "expected_usd",
                             "bank_total", "total"]])
    usd_field = F.coalesce(*[F.col(c).cast("double") for c in
                             ["amount_usd", "usd_amount"]])

    return df.select(
        rec_type.alias("rec_type"),
        src.alias("src"),
        ccy.alias("ccy"),
        amt.alias("amt"),
        rate.alias("rate"),
        sdate.alias("settlement_date"),
        bank_usd.alias("bank_usd"),
        usd_field.alias("usd_field"),
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="fx_rates_daily")
def fx_rates_daily():
    b = SparkSession.active().read.table("settlement_bronze")
    fx = b.where(
        (F.col("rec_type").rlike("rate|fx"))
        | (F.col("rate").isNotNull() & F.col("amt").isNull()
           & F.col("bank_usd").isNull())
    ).where(
        F.col("ccy").isNotNull() & F.col("settlement_date").isNotNull()
        & F.col("rate").isNotNull()
    )
    # deterministic pick of the rate effective that day per currency
    return fx.groupBy("settlement_date", "ccy").agg(
        F.max("rate").alias("daily_rate"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="payments_usd")
def payments_usd():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    b = spark.read.table("settlement_bronze")
    pay = b.where(
        (~F.col("rec_type").rlike("rate|fx|extern|bank|recon"))
        & F.col("amt").isNotNull() & F.col("ccy").isNotNull()
        & F.col("settlement_date").isNotNull()
    )
    fx = spark.read.table("fx_rates_daily")
    j = pay.join(fx, ["settlement_date", "ccy"], "left")

    eff = F.when(F.col("ccy") == F.lit("USD"), F.lit(1.0)) \
        .otherwise(F.coalesce(F.col("rate"), F.col("daily_rate")))
    value_usd = F.round(F.col("amt") * eff, 2)

    return j.select(
        F.col("settlement_date"),
        F.col("ccy"),
        F.col("amt"),
        value_usd.alias("value_usd"),
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="settlement_daily_by_currency")
def settlement_daily_by_currency():
    p = SparkSession.active().read.table("payments_usd")
    return p.groupBy("settlement_date", "ccy").agg(
        F.round(F.sum("amt"), 2).alias("currency_total"),
        F.round(F.sum("value_usd"), 2).alias("usd_total"),
    ).select(
        F.col("settlement_date"),
        F.col("ccy").alias("currency"),
        F.col("currency_total"),
        F.col("usd_total"),
    )


@dp.materialized_view(name="settlement_daily")
def settlement_daily():
    bc = SparkSession.active().read.table("settlement_daily_by_currency")
    return bc.groupBy("settlement_date").agg(
        F.round(F.sum("usd_total"), 2).alias("usd_total"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="external_settlements_daily")
def external_settlements_daily():
    b = SparkSession.active().read.table("settlement_bronze")
    ext = b.where(
        F.col("rec_type").rlike("extern|bank|recon")
        | F.col("src").rlike("extern|bank")
    ).where(F.col("ccy").isNotNull() & F.col("settlement_date").isNotNull())

    ext_usd = F.coalesce(
        F.col("bank_usd"),
        F.col("usd_field"),
        F.when(F.col("ccy") == F.lit("USD"), F.col("amt")),
        F.col("amt") * F.col("rate"),
    )
    return ext.select(
        F.col("settlement_date"),
        F.col("ccy").alias("currency"),
        ext_usd.alias("usd_bank"),
    ).groupBy("settlement_date", "currency").agg(
        F.round(F.sum("usd_bank"), 2).alias("usd_total_bank"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="settlement_reconciliation")
def settlement_reconciliation():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    ours = spark.read.table("settlement_daily_by_currency").select(
        "settlement_date", "currency",
        F.col("usd_total").alias("usd_total_ours"))
    bank = spark.read.table("external_settlements_daily")

    recon = ours.join(bank, ["settlement_date", "currency"], "full_outer")
    diff = F.round(
        F.coalesce(F.col("usd_total_ours"), F.lit(0.0))
        - F.coalesce(F.col("usd_total_bank"), F.lit(0.0)), 2)
    return recon.select(
        F.col("settlement_date"),
        F.col("currency"),
        F.coalesce(F.col("usd_total_ours"), F.lit(0.0)).alias("usd_total_ours"),
        F.coalesce(F.col("usd_total_bank"), F.lit(0.0)).alias("usd_total_bank"),
        diff.alias("difference"),
        (F.abs(diff) < F.lit(0.01)).alias("matched"),
    )

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, types as T
from pyspark.sql import Window

DATA_PATH = ("file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/"
             ".work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_payments_seed16180.ndjson")

_S = T.StringType()
_D = T.DoubleType()
def _f(n, t): return T.StructField(n, t, True)

# Broad, explicit schema: every candidate field name is declared so column
# resolution never fails at dry-run. Absent feeds simply read as null.
SCHEMA = T.StructType([
    _f("source", _S), _f("record_type", _S), _f("type", _S), _f("feed", _S), _f("kind", _S),
    _f("ts", _S), _f("timestamp", _S), _f("event_time", _S), _f("event_ts", _S),
    _f("created_at", _S), _f("time", _S),
    _f("date", _S), _f("settlement_date", _S), _f("value_date", _S),
    _f("rate_date", _S), _f("effective_date", _S),
    _f("currency", _S), _f("ccy", _S), _f("currency_code", _S),
    _f("amount", _D), _f("value", _D), _f("amount_native", _D),
    _f("native_amount", _D), _f("settled_amount", _D),
    _f("fx_rate", _D), _f("rate", _D), _f("fx_rate_to_usd", _D),
    _f("rate_to_usd", _D), _f("usd_rate", _D),
    _f("amount_usd", _D), _f("usd_amount", _D), _f("usd_value", _D),
    _f("settled_usd", _D), _f("usd", _D),
    _f("ts_ms", _D), _f("ts_millis", _D), _f("epoch_ms", _D), _f("ts_s", _D),
])

def _disc():
    return F.lower(F.coalesce(F.col("source"), F.col("record_type"), F.col("type"),
                              F.col("feed"), F.col("kind"), F.lit("internal")))

def _currency():
    return F.upper(F.coalesce(F.col("currency"), F.col("ccy"), F.col("currency_code")))

def _native():
    return F.coalesce(F.col("amount"), F.col("value"), F.col("amount_native"),
                      F.col("native_amount"), F.col("settled_amount"))

def _rate():
    return F.coalesce(F.col("fx_rate"), F.col("rate"), F.col("fx_rate_to_usd"),
                      F.col("rate_to_usd"), F.col("usd_rate"))

def _usd_direct():
    return F.coalesce(F.col("amount_usd"), F.col("usd_amount"), F.col("usd_value"),
                      F.col("settled_usd"), F.col("usd"))

def _utc_date():
    # UTC calendar day, tz-independent: prefer an explicit date, else derive it
    # from the absolute epoch (no session-timezone dependency, no conf mutation).
    ts_str = F.coalesce(F.col("ts"), F.col("timestamp"), F.col("event_time"),
                        F.col("event_ts"), F.col("created_at"), F.col("time"))
    epoch_ms = F.coalesce(F.col("ts_ms"), F.col("ts_millis"), F.col("epoch_ms"))
    epoch_s = F.coalesce(F.unix_timestamp(F.to_timestamp(ts_str)),
                         epoch_ms / F.lit(1000.0), F.col("ts_s"))
    day = F.floor(epoch_s / F.lit(86400.0)).cast("int")
    date_from_epoch = F.date_add(F.to_date(F.lit("1970-01-01")), day)
    date_str = F.coalesce(F.col("date"), F.col("settlement_date"), F.col("value_date"),
                          F.col("rate_date"), F.col("effective_date"))
    return F.coalesce(F.to_date(date_str), date_from_epoch)


@dp.materialized_view(name="raw_events")
def raw_events():
    return SparkSession.active().read.schema(SCHEMA).json(DATA_PATH)


@dp.materialized_view(name="fx_rates")
def fx_rates():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table("spark_catalog.default.raw_events")
    return (df.withColumn("currency", _currency())
              .withColumn("rate_dt", _utc_date())
              .withColumn("fx", _rate())
              .where(F.col("fx").isNotNull() & F.col("currency").isNotNull()
                     & F.col("rate_dt").isNotNull())
              .groupBy("currency", "rate_dt")
              .agg(F.max("fx").alias("fx_rate"))
              .select(F.col("currency").alias("r_currency"),
                      F.col("rate_dt"), F.col("fx_rate")))


@dp.materialized_view(name="settlement_by_currency")
def settlement_by_currency():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    df = spark.read.table("spark_catalog.default.raw_events")
    pay = (df.withColumn("disc", _disc())
             .withColumn("currency", _currency())
             .withColumn("settlement_date", _utc_date())
             .withColumn("native", _native())
             .withColumn("usd_direct", _usd_direct())
             .where(~F.col("disc").rlike("bank|external"))
             .where(F.col("currency").isNotNull() & F.col("settlement_date").isNotNull()
                    & (F.col("native").isNotNull() | F.col("usd_direct").isNotNull())))
    agg = (pay.groupBy("settlement_date", "currency")
              .agg(F.sum("native").alias("native_sum"),
                   F.sum("usd_direct").alias("usd_direct_sum")))
    rates = spark.read.table("spark_catalog.default.fx_rates")
    # as-of join: rate effective that day = latest rate on/before the settlement day
    j = agg.join(rates,
                 (agg["currency"] == rates["r_currency"]) &
                 (rates["rate_dt"] <= agg["settlement_date"]), "left")
    w = Window.partitionBy(agg["settlement_date"], agg["currency"]).orderBy(
        rates["rate_dt"].desc_nulls_last(), rates["fx_rate"].desc_nulls_last())
    picked = j.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w)).where(F.col("_rn") == 1)
    return picked.select(
        agg["settlement_date"].alias("settlement_date"),
        agg["currency"].alias("currency"),
        F.round(
            F.when(F.col("native_sum").isNotNull() & F.col("fx_rate").isNotNull(),
                   F.col("native_sum") * F.col("fx_rate"))
             .otherwise(F.col("usd_direct_sum")), 2).alias("usd_amount"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="settlement_daily")
def settlement_daily():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table("spark_catalog.default.settlement_by_currency")
    return (df.groupBy("settlement_date")
              .agg(F.round(F.sum("usd_amount"), 2).alias("usd_total")))


@dp.materialized_view(name="external_settlements")
def external_settlements():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table("spark_catalog.default.raw_events")
    ext = (df.withColumn("disc", _disc())
             .withColumn("currency", _currency())
             .withColumn("settlement_date", _utc_date())
             .withColumn("bank_usd_line", F.coalesce(_usd_direct(), _native()))
             .where(F.col("disc").rlike("bank|external"))
             .where(F.col("currency").isNotNull() & F.col("settlement_date").isNotNull()))
    return (ext.groupBy("settlement_date", "currency")
               .agg(F.round(F.sum("bank_usd_line"), 2).alias("bank_usd")))


@dp.materialized_view(name="settlement_reconciliation")
def settlement_reconciliation():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    ours = spark.read.table("spark_catalog.default.settlement_by_currency")
    bank = spark.read.table("spark_catalog.default.external_settlements")
    j = ours.join(bank, ["settlement_date", "currency"], "full_outer")
    recon = j.select(
        F.col("settlement_date"), F.col("currency"),
        F.coalesce(F.col("usd_amount"), F.lit(0.0)).alias("_our"),
        F.coalesce(F.col("bank_usd"), F.lit(0.0)).alias("_bank"),
        F.col("usd_amount").isNull().alias("_missing_ours"),
        F.col("bank_usd").isNull().alias("_missing_bank"))
    return recon.select(
        "settlement_date", "currency",
        F.round(F.col("_our"), 2).alias("our_usd"),
        F.round(F.col("_bank"), 2).alias("bank_usd"),
        F.round(F.col("_our") - F.col("_bank"), 2).alias("diff_usd"),
        F.when(F.col("_missing_ours"), F.lit("MISSING_IN_OURS"))
         .when(F.col("_missing_bank"), F.lit("MISSING_IN_BANK"))
         .when(F.abs(F.col("_our") - F.col("_bank")) < F.lit(0.01), F.lit("MATCH"))
         .otherwise(F.lit("MISMATCH")).alias("status"))

from pyspark import pipelines as dp
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F
from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, StringType, DoubleType

DATA = "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_payments_seed14142.ndjson"
CAT = "spark_catalog.default."

# Explicit schema => no eager inference job (avoids ATTEMPT_ANALYSIS) and any
# field absent from the JSON resolves to NULL instead of UNRESOLVED_COLUMN.
SCHEMA = StructType([
    StructField("payment_id", StringType()),
    StructField("event_id", StringType()),
    StructField("id", StringType()),
    StructField("record_type", StringType()),
    StructField("type", StringType()),
    StructField("source", StringType()),
    StructField("feed", StringType()),
    StructField("origin", StringType()),
    StructField("event_ts", StringType()),
    StructField("timestamp", StringType()),
    StructField("ts", StringType()),
    StructField("event_time", StringType()),
    StructField("created_at", StringType()),
    StructField("settlement_date", StringType()),
    StructField("date", StringType()),
    StructField("day", StringType()),
    StructField("value_date", StringType()),
    StructField("currency", StringType()),
    StructField("ccy", StringType()),
    StructField("currency_code", StringType()),
    StructField("amount", DoubleType()),
    StructField("value", DoubleType()),
    StructField("settled_amount", DoubleType()),
    StructField("amount_usd", DoubleType()),
    StructField("usd_amount", DoubleType()),
    StructField("usd", DoubleType()),
    StructField("settled_usd", DoubleType()),
    StructField("fx_rate", DoubleType()),
    StructField("rate", DoubleType()),
    StructField("rate_to_usd", DoubleType()),
    StructField("fx_rate_to_usd", DoubleType()),
    StructField("status", StringType()),
])


@dp.materialized_view(name="settlement_raw")
def settlement_raw():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.schema(SCHEMA).json(DATA)

    # UTC calendar day: to_date over the messy date/timestamp candidates.
    sdate = F.coalesce(
        F.to_date("settlement_date"),
        F.to_date("value_date"),
        F.to_date("date"),
        F.to_date("day"),
        F.to_date(F.col("event_ts")),
        F.to_date(F.col("timestamp")),
        F.to_date(F.col("ts")),
        F.to_date(F.col("event_time")),
        F.to_date(F.col("created_at")),
    )
    ccy = F.upper(F.coalesce(F.col("currency"), F.col("ccy"), F.col("currency_code")))
    amount = F.coalesce(F.col("amount"), F.col("value"), F.col("settled_amount"))
    rate = F.coalesce(F.col("fx_rate"), F.col("rate"), F.col("rate_to_usd"),
                      F.col("fx_rate_to_usd"), F.lit(1.0))
    # USD converted at the rate effective that day (per-record rate), or an
    # explicit USD field when the feed already carries it.
    usd = F.coalesce(F.col("amount_usd"), F.col("usd_amount"), F.col("usd"),
                     F.col("settled_usd"), amount * rate)
    kind = F.lower(F.coalesce(F.col("record_type"), F.col("type"), F.col("source"),
                              F.col("feed"), F.col("origin"), F.lit("internal")))
    is_external = kind.rlike("bank|extern|settle")

    return (df
        .withColumn("settlement_date", sdate)
        .withColumn("currency", ccy)
        .withColumn("amount_ccy", amount)
        .withColumn("usd_value", usd)
        .withColumn("is_external", is_external)
        .where(F.col("settlement_date").isNotNull() & F.col("currency").isNotNull())
        .select("settlement_date", "currency", "amount_ccy", "usd_value", "is_external"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="internal_settlements")
def internal_settlements():
    return SparkSession.active().read.table(CAT + "settlement_raw").where(~F.col("is_external"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="external_settlements")
def external_settlements():
    return SparkSession.active().read.table(CAT + "settlement_raw").where(F.col("is_external"))


@dp.materialized_view(name="settlement_daily")
def settlement_daily():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table(CAT + "internal_settlements")
    return (df.groupBy("settlement_date")
              .agg(F.round(F.sum("usd_value"), 2).alias("usd_total")))


@dp.materialized_view(name="settlement_by_currency")
def settlement_by_currency():
    df = SparkSession.active().read.table(CAT + "internal_settlements")
    return (df.groupBy("settlement_date", "currency")
              .agg(F.round(F.sum("amount_ccy"), 2).alias("currency_total"),
                   F.round(F.sum("usd_value"), 2).alias("usd_total")))


@dp.materialized_view(name="settlement_reconciliation")
def settlement_reconciliation():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    internal = (spark.read.table(CAT + "internal_settlements")
                .groupBy("settlement_date", "currency")
                .agg(F.round(F.sum("usd_value"), 2).alias("internal_usd")))
    external = (spark.read.table(CAT + "external_settlements")
                .groupBy("settlement_date", "currency")
                .agg(F.round(F.sum("usd_value"), 2).alias("external_usd")))
    j = internal.join(external, ["settlement_date", "currency"], "full_outer")
    return (j
        .withColumn("internal_usd", F.coalesce(F.col("internal_usd"), F.lit(0.0)))
        .withColumn("external_usd", F.coalesce(F.col("external_usd"), F.lit(0.0)))
        .withColumn("diff", F.round(F.col("internal_usd") - F.col("external_usd"), 2))
        .withColumn("matched", F.abs(F.col("diff")) < F.lit(0.01)))

#!/usr/bin/env python
"""pipeline.py -- idempotent upsert into keyed silver_orders.

Ticket: nightly load re-reads overlapping windows of the `orders` feed, so the
same order_id keeps getting appended and totals drift. We must produce a table
keyed by order_id with EXACTLY one row per order, reflecting the order's real
values, and stable across arbitrary re-runs / replays.

Strategy:
  * read the raw feed defensively (everything nullable, amount as string)
  * clean `amount` -> double (strip currency symbols / commas / blanks) instead
    of a naive cast that blows up with CAST_INVALID_INPUT
  * collapse duplicates deterministically: for each order_id keep the "latest,
    most-complete" record using whatever ordering signal the feed carries
    (event/version/seq/timestamp), with deterministic tie-breaks so replaying
    the same input can never change the result.
"""

import os
import sys

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window


DEFAULT_INPUT = (
    "/home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/"
    ".work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed42.ndjson"
)


def get_input_path() -> str:
    return os.environ.get("AGENT_INPUT_PATH", DEFAULT_INPUT)


def clean_amount(col):
    """Coerce a messy amount value to double without raising CAST_INVALID_INPUT.

    Keeps only digits, dot and leading minus; empty results become NULL.
    """
    stripped = F.regexp_replace(F.col(col).cast("string"), r"[^0-9.\-]", "")
    stripped = F.when(F.trim(stripped) == F.lit(""), F.lit(None)).otherwise(stripped)
    # try_cast-like behaviour: bad content -> NULL rather than an error
    return stripped.cast("double")


def build_silver(spark: SparkSession, input_path: str):
    # Read everything as-is; do NOT impose a numeric schema that could throw.
    raw = spark.read.json(input_path)
    cols = set(raw.columns)

    if "order_id" not in cols:
        raise ValueError(f"input feed missing order_id; columns={sorted(cols)}")

    df = raw

    # Normalise the payload columns we contractually carry.
    df = df.withColumn("amount", clean_amount("amount") if "amount" in cols else F.lit(None).cast("double"))
    df = df.withColumn(
        "category",
        F.trim(F.col("category").cast("string")) if "category" in cols else F.lit(None).cast("string"),
    )

    # order_id: normalise to a clean string key, drop rows with no key.
    df = df.withColumn("order_id", F.trim(F.col("order_id").cast("string")))
    df = df.filter(F.col("order_id").isNotNull() & (F.col("order_id") != ""))

    # Pick a recency / version signal if the feed provides one. This lets a
    # later replay of an updated order win, while remaining deterministic.
    order_candidates = [
        "updated_at", "update_ts", "event_time", "event_ts", "timestamp",
        "ts", "version", "seq", "sequence", "revision", "batch_id", "load_ts",
    ]
    recency_cols = [c for c in order_candidates if c in cols]

    order_by = []
    for c in recency_cols:
        # cast to string for a stable, type-safe ordering key
        order_by.append(F.col(c).cast("string").desc_nulls_last())
    # Prefer records that actually carry an amount, then deterministic tie-breaks.
    order_by.append(F.col("amount").isNotNull().desc())
    order_by.append(F.col("amount").desc_nulls_last())
    order_by.append(F.col("category").desc_nulls_last())

    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(*order_by)
    deduped = (
        df.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
          .filter(F.col("_rn") == 1)
          .drop("_rn")
    )

    silver = deduped.select(
        F.col("order_id").cast("string").alias("order_id"),
        F.col("amount").cast("double").alias("amount"),
        F.col("category").cast("string").alias("category"),
    )
    return silver


def main() -> int:
    spark = (
        SparkSession.builder
        .appName("new_merge_upsert")
        .getOrCreate()
    )
    spark.sparkContext.setLogLevel("WARN")

    input_path = get_input_path()
    silver = build_silver(spark, input_path)

    # Register the contract table as a temp view in this session.
    silver.createOrReplaceTempView("silver_orders")

    out_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH")
    out_table = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE")

    if out_path:
        # Overwrite => idempotent: replaying overlapping batches yields the
        # same one-row-per-order result every time.
        silver.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(out_path)
    elif out_table:
        silver.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable(out_table)
    else:
        raise ValueError("neither AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH nor AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE is set")

    print("Run is COMPLETED")
    spark.stop()
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

import os
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, Window
from pyspark.sql import functions as F


def build_spark():
    return (
        SparkSession.builder
        .appName("new_merge_upsert")
        .master(os.environ.get("SPARK_MASTER", "local[*]"))
        .getOrCreate()
    )


def clean_amount(col):
    # amount may arrive as a string with currency symbols, thousands
    # separators, stray whitespace, or plain junk. Strip everything that is
    # not a digit / dot / minus, then cast. Empty -> NULL (invalid -> NULL
    # instead of blowing up with CAST_INVALID_INPUT).
    s = F.regexp_replace(col.cast("string"), r"[^0-9.\-]", "")
    return F.when(F.length(s) == 0, F.lit(None)).otherwise(s.cast("double"))


def main():
    spark = build_spark()
    spark.conf.set("spark.sql.session.timeZone", "UTC")

    in_path = os.environ.get(
        "AGENT_INPUT_PATH",
        "/home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/"
        ".work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed1337.ndjson",
    )
    out_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH")
    out_table = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE")

    raw = spark.read.option("mode", "PERMISSIVE").json(in_path)
    cols = set(raw.columns)

    # order_id may be typed as long/string; normalize to string key, drop nulls.
    df = raw.withColumn("order_id", F.col("order_id").cast("string"))
    df = df.filter(F.col("order_id").isNotNull() & (F.length(F.trim(F.col("order_id"))) > 0))

    # Robust amount + category.
    df = df.withColumn("amount_clean", clean_amount(F.col("amount")))
    if "category" in cols:
        df = df.withColumn("category_clean", F.col("category").cast("string"))
    else:
        df = df.withColumn("category_clean", F.lit(None).cast("string"))

    # Deterministic "latest / real value" ordering. Use any monotonic
    # version/time column the feed happens to carry so re-reads of overlapping
    # batches collapse to the same winning row.
    order_candidates = [
        "event_time", "event_ts", "event_timestamp", "ts", "timestamp",
        "updated_at", "update_time", "ingest_time", "ingested_at",
        "load_ts", "_ingest_ts", "version", "seq", "sequence",
    ]
    order_col = next((c for c in order_candidates if c in cols), None)

    order_by = [F.col("amount_clean").isNotNull().desc()]
    if order_col is not None:
        order_by.append(F.col(order_col).cast("string").desc_nulls_last())
    # Fully deterministic tie-breakers so reprocessing never changes result.
    order_by += [
        F.col("amount_clean").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("category_clean").asc_nulls_last(),
    ]

    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(*order_by)
    deduped = (
        df.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
          .filter(F.col("_rn") == 1)
          .drop("_rn")
    )

    silver = deduped.select(
        F.col("order_id"),
        F.col("amount_clean").alias("amount"),
        F.col("category_clean").alias("category"),
    )

    silver.createOrReplaceTempView("silver_orders")

    if out_path:
        (silver.coalesce(1)
               .write.mode("overwrite")
               .parquet(out_path))
    elif out_table:
        (silver.write.mode("overwrite")
               .format("parquet")
               .saveAsTable(out_table))
    else:
        raise RuntimeError("No output location provided (AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH / AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE).")

    print("Run is COMPLETED")
    spark.stop()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

"""
pipeline.py -- idempotent merge/upsert transform for the messy `orders` feed.

Problem: the nightly load re-reads overlapping windows of the `orders` feed, so
the same order shows up in multiple replayed batches. The keyed output table must
converge to EXACTLY one row per order_id, reflecting the order's real values, and
must be stable no matter how many overlapping batches are replayed.

Strategy:
  * Read the raw NDJSON (schema is genuinely messy).
  * Defensively parse `amount` (strip currency/formatting junk, tolerate bad
    values instead of blowing up on ANSI CAST_INVALID_INPUT).
  * Collapse duplicates deterministically to one row per order_id, preferring the
    most recent / most complete observation. Because the pick is deterministic and
    we overwrite the output, replaying the same (or overlapping) input always
    yields the same result -> safe to re-run.
"""

import os
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window

DEFAULT_INPUT = (
    "/home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/"
    ".work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed2718.ndjson"
)


def get_spark():
    return (
        SparkSession.builder
        .appName("new_merge_upsert")
        # Our own session: keep casts lenient so messy amounts become NULL
        # instead of raising CAST_INVALID_INPUT.
        .config("spark.sql.ansi.enabled", "false")
        .getOrCreate()
    )


def build_silver_orders(df):
    cols = df.columns

    # --- normalize the key -------------------------------------------------
    key = F.trim(F.col("order_id").cast("string"))
    df = df.withColumn("order_id", key).filter(
        F.col("order_id").isNotNull() & (F.col("order_id") != "")
    )

    # --- defensively parse amount -----------------------------------------
    # Strip anything that is not part of a number (currency symbols, commas,
    # whitespace, stray text). Empty -> NULL so nothing raises on cast.
    amount_str = F.regexp_replace(F.col("amount").cast("string"), r"[^0-9eE.+\-]", "")
    amount_clean = F.when(F.trim(amount_str) == "", None).otherwise(
        amount_str.cast("double")
    )
    df = df.withColumn("amount", amount_clean)

    # --- normalize category ------------------------------------------------
    if "category" in cols:
        df = df.withColumn(
            "category",
            F.when(F.trim(F.col("category").cast("string")) == "", None).otherwise(
                F.trim(F.col("category").cast("string"))
            ),
        )
    else:
        df = df.withColumn("category", F.lit(None).cast("string"))

    # --- deterministic dedup to one row per order_id -----------------------
    # Prefer the most recent observation if the feed carries a recency signal;
    # otherwise fall back to a fully deterministic ordering so replays are stable.
    recency_candidates = [
        "updated_at", "update_ts", "event_time", "event_ts", "timestamp",
        "ts", "seq", "sequence", "version", "batch", "batch_id",
        "ingest_ts", "_ingest_ts", "load_ts", "row_ts",
    ]
    recency_col = next((c for c in recency_candidates if c in cols), None)

    order_by = []
    if recency_col is not None:
        order_by.append(F.col(recency_col).desc_nulls_last())
    # Prefer a row that actually has an amount, then a stable, reproducible tie-break.
    order_by += [
        F.col("amount").isNotNull().desc(),
        F.col("amount").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("category").asc_nulls_last(),
    ]

    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(*order_by)
    deduped = (
        df.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
        .filter(F.col("_rn") == 1)
        .select("order_id", "amount", "category")
    )

    return deduped


def main():
    spark = get_spark()
    spark.sparkContext.setLogLevel("WARN")

    input_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_INPUT_PATH", DEFAULT_INPUT)
    df = spark.read.json(input_path)

    silver_orders = build_silver_orders(df)

    # Register as a temp view under the exact contract name.
    silver_orders.createOrReplaceTempView("silver_orders")

    out_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH")
    out_table = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE")

    if out_path:
        # Overwrite makes re-runs idempotent: same input -> same materialized output.
        silver_orders.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(out_path)

import os
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window


def build_silver_orders(spark, input_path):
    # Raw messy feed: same order_id can appear many times across replayed batches.
    df = spark.read.json(input_path)
    cols = set(df.columns)

    # --- Defensive amount cleaning (avoid CAST_INVALID_INPUT on dirty strings) ---
    # amount may arrive as number, or as string like "$1,234.50", "USD 12", "", "n/a"
    amt_str = F.col("amount").cast("string")
    amt_clean = F.regexp_replace(amt_str, r"[^0-9.\-]", "")
    amount_col = (
        F.when(amt_clean.isNull() | (amt_clean == "") | (amt_clean == "-") | (amt_clean == "."), None)
        .otherwise(amt_clean.cast("double"))
    )
    df = df.withColumn("amount", amount_col)

    # category as trimmed string
    if "category" in cols:
        df = df.withColumn("category", F.trim(F.col("category").cast("string")))
    else:
        df = df.withColumn("category", F.lit(None).cast("string"))

    # keep order_id exactly as delivered (do not force-cast the key)
    # --- Deterministic ordering so replay picks the SAME "real" row every time ---
    order_candidates = [
        "updated_at", "update_ts", "updated_ts", "modified_at",
        "event_time", "event_ts", "event_timestamp",
        "ts", "timestamp", "ingest_ts", "_ingest_ts",
        "version", "seq", "sequence", "offset", "batch_id",
    ]
    order_by = []
    for c in order_candidates:
        if c in cols:
            # latest wins; cast to string keeps ordering deterministic for mixed types
            order_by.append(F.col(c).cast("string").desc_nulls_last())

    # Tie-breakers make the choice fully deterministic (stable across re-runs):
    # prefer a real (non-null) amount, then largest amount, then category.
    order_by.append(F.col("amount").isNull().asc())
    order_by.append(F.col("amount").desc_nulls_last())
    order_by.append(F.col("category").asc_nulls_last())

    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(*order_by)
    ranked = df.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))

    result = (
        ranked.filter(F.col("_rn") == 1)
        .select("order_id", "amount", "category")
    )
    return result


def main():
    spark = (
        SparkSession.builder
        .appName("new_merge_upsert")
        .getOrCreate()
    )

    default_input = (
        "/home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/"
        ".work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed3141.ndjson"
    )
    input_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_INPUT_PATH", default_input)

    result = build_silver_orders(spark, input_path)

    # Register the contract view in this SparkSession.
    result.createOrReplaceTempView("silver_orders")

    out_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH")
    out_table = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE")

    if out_path:
        # Idempotent overwrite -> re-running yields identical materialized output.
        result.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(out_path)
    elif out_table:
        result.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable(out_table)
    else:
        raise RuntimeError("No output location provided (AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH / AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE).")

    print("Run is COMPLETED")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window


CANDIDATE_ORDER_COLS = [
    "version", "seq", "sequence", "batch_id",
    "event_time", "updated_at", "updated_ts", "modified_at",
    "ts", "timestamp", "created_at", "ingest_time", "load_time",
]

DEFAULT_INPUT = (
    "/home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/"
    ".work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed8675.ndjson"
)


def build_spark():
    return (
        SparkSession.builder
        .appName("new_merge_upsert")
        .config("spark.sql.session.timeZone", "UTC")
        .getOrCreate()
    )


def read_input(spark, path):
    # NDJSON: one JSON object per line
    return spark.read.json(path)


def dedup_to_one_row_per_order(df):
    cols = set(df.columns)
    if "order_id" not in cols:
        raise ValueError(f"'order_id' not present in input columns: {sorted(cols)}")

    # Normalize the payload columns to their contract types.
    amount_col = (
        F.col("amount").cast("double") if "amount" in cols else F.lit(None).cast("double")
    )
    category_col = (
        F.col("category").cast("string") if "category" in cols else F.lit(None).cast("string")
    )

    normalized = df.select(
        F.col("order_id").alias("order_id"),
        amount_col.alias("amount"),
        category_col.alias("category"),
        *[c for c in df.columns if c not in ("order_id", "amount", "category")],
    )

    # Build a deterministic ordering: latest version wins, with full tie-breakers
    # so the chosen row is a pure function of the data (idempotent across replays).
    present_order_cols = [c for c in CANDIDATE_ORDER_COLS if c in normalized.columns]

    ordering = [F.col(c).desc_nulls_last() for c in present_order_cols]
    # Deterministic tie-breakers over the payload itself.
    for c in ["amount", "category"]:
        ordering.append(F.col(c).desc_nulls_last())
    # Any remaining columns as final tie-breakers for full determinism.
    for c in normalized.columns:
        if c not in present_order_cols + ["order_id", "amount", "category"]:
            ordering.append(F.col(c).cast("string").desc_nulls_last())

    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(*ordering)

    deduped = (
        normalized
        .withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
        .filter(F.col("_rn") == 1)
        .drop("_rn")
    )

    # Exact contract projection + grain.
    return deduped.select("order_id", "amount", "category")


def write_output(silver):
    out_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH")
    out_table = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE")

    if out_path:
        (
            silver.coalesce(1)
            .write.mode("overwrite")
            .parquet(out_path)
        )
    elif out_table:
        (
            silver.write.mode("overwrite")
            .format("parquet")
            .saveAsTable(out_table)
        )
    else:
        raise ValueError("Neither AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH nor AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE is set")


def main():
    spark = build_spark()
    try:
        input_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_INPUT_PATH", DEFAULT_INPUT)

        raw = read_input(spark, input_path)
        silver = dedup_to_one_row_per_order(raw)

        # Register as a temp view under the contract name for cross-table consistency.
        silver.createOrReplaceTempView("silver_orders")

        write_output(silver)

        print("Run is COMPLETED")
    finally:
        spark.stop()

    main()

import os
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window

# Default dataset for this seed (used only if AGENT_INPUT_PATH is unset).
DEFAULT_INPUT = (
    "/home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/"
    ".work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed9001.ndjson"
)

# Candidate columns that carry an ordering/recency signal, best first.
ORDER_CANDIDATES = [
    "event_time", "event_ts", "event_timestamp",
    "updated_at", "update_ts", "updated_ts", "modified_at",
    "ingest_ts", "ingested_at", "_ingested_at",
    "timestamp", "ts", "time",
    "version", "ver", "revision",
    "seq", "sequence", "offset", "batch", "batch_id",
]


def main():
    spark = (
        SparkSession.builder
        .appName("new_merge_upsert")
        .getOrCreate()
    )
    spark.sparkContext.setLogLevel("WARN")

    input_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_INPUT_PATH", DEFAULT_INPUT)
    output_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH")
    output_table = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE")

    raw = spark.read.json(input_path)
    cols = set(raw.columns)

    # ---- Locate the key column -------------------------------------------
    if "order_id" not in cols:
        # tolerate common aliases
        for alias in ("orderId", "orderid", "id", "order"):
            if alias in cols:
                raw = raw.withColumnRenamed(alias, "order_id")
                cols = set(raw.columns)
                break
    if "order_id" not in raw.columns:
        raise ValueError("input has no order_id column; cannot satisfy contract")

    # ---- Locate amount / category (tolerate aliases) ---------------------
    if "amount" not in raw.columns:
        for alias in ("amt", "total", "value", "price"):
            if alias in raw.columns:
                raw = raw.withColumnRenamed(alias, "amount")
                break
    if "category" not in raw.columns:
        for alias in ("cat", "category_name", "type", "product_category"):
            if alias in raw.columns:
                raw = raw.withColumnRenamed(alias, "category")
                break

    for required in ("amount", "category"):
        if required not in raw.columns:
            raw = raw.withColumn(required, F.lit(None))

    # ---- Normalize payload (clean, do not invent) ------------------------
    # amount -> double, stripping any currency/formatting noise from strings.
    amount_str = F.col("amount").cast("string")
    amount_clean = F.regexp_replace(amount_str, r"[^0-9.\-]", "")
    amount_clean = F.when(amount_clean == "", None).otherwise(amount_clean)

    df = (
        raw
        .withColumn("order_id", F.col("order_id").cast("string"))
        .withColumn("amount", amount_clean.cast("double"))
        .withColumn(
            "category",
            F.when(F.trim(F.col("category").cast("string")) == "", None)
             .otherwise(F.trim(F.col("category").cast("string"))),
        )
        .filter(F.col("order_id").isNotNull() & (F.trim(F.col("order_id")) != ""))
    )

    # ---- Deterministic merge/upsert per order_id -------------------------
    # Survivor preference (all deterministic, independent of arrival order):
    #   1. rows whose amount & category are both present (real values)
    #   2. latest by any detected recency column(s)
    #   3. full-payload tiebreak so replays are byte-stable
    completeness = (
        F.when(F.col("amount").isNotNull() & F.col("category").isNotNull(), 2)
         .when(F.col("amount").isNotNull() | F.col("category").isNotNull(), 1)
         .otherwise(0)
    )

    order_by = [completeness.desc()]
    for c in ORDER_CANDIDATES:
        if c in df.columns:
            order_by.append(F.col(c).desc_nulls_last())
    # total, content-based tiebreak (guarantees a single deterministic winner)
    order_by += [
        F.col("amount").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("category").desc_nulls_last(),
    ]

    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(*order_by)

    deduped = (
        df.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
          .filter(F.col("_rn") == 1)
          .select("order_id", "amount", "category")
    )

    # Register as the contract-named temp view in this session.
    deduped.createOrReplaceTempView("silver_orders")

    # ---- Materialize GOLD ------------------------------------------------
    if output_path:
        deduped.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(output_path)
    elif output_table:
        deduped.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable(output_table)
    else:
        raise ValueError("no AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH or AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE provided")

    print("Run is COMPLETED")
    spark.stop()

    main()

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window


def get_spark():
    return (
        SparkSession.builder
        .appName("new_merge_upsert")
        .master(os.environ.get("SPARK_MASTER", "local[*]"))
        .getOrCreate()
    )


def main():
    spark = get_spark()
    spark.sparkContext.setLogLevel("WARN")

    default_input = "/home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed11235.ndjson"
    input_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_INPUT_PATH", default_input)

    out_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH")
    out_table = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE")

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Read raw messy feed (schema-inferred). Everything read as-is.
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    raw = spark.read.json(input_path)
    cols = set(raw.columns)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Normalize order_id -> string key
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    if "order_id" not in cols:
        # fall back to any id-like column
        candidate = next((c for c in raw.columns if "id" in c.lower()), raw.columns[0])
        raw = raw.withColumnRenamed(candidate, "order_id")

    df = raw.withColumn("order_id", F.trim(F.col("order_id").cast("string")))

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Clean amount: strip currency symbols / commas / whitespace, then
    # safely cast. Never let a bad string blow up the run.
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    if "amount" not in df.columns:
        amt_col = next((c for c in df.columns if "amount" in c.lower() or "amt" in c.lower() or "total" in c.lower()), None)
        if amt_col:
            df = df.withColumnRenamed(amt_col, "amount")
        else:
            df = df.withColumn("amount", F.lit(None).cast("string"))

    amount_str = F.col("amount").cast("string")
    cleaned = F.regexp_replace(amount_str, r"[^0-9.\-]", "")
    # collapse anything that isn't a valid decimal to null via try cast
    df = df.withColumn(
        "amount",
        F.when(F.length(cleaned) == 0, F.lit(None).cast("double"))
         .otherwise(F.expr("try_cast(regexp_replace(cast(amount as string), '[^0-9.\\\\-]', '') as double)"))
    )

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # category -> string
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    if "category" not in df.columns:
        cat_col = next((c for c in df.columns if "categ" in c.lower()), None)
        if cat_col:
            df = df.withColumnRenamed(cat_col, "category")
        else:
            df = df.withColumn("category", F.lit(None).cast("string"))
    df = df.withColumn("category", F.col("category").cast("string"))

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Deterministic dedup: exactly one row per order_id.
    # Prefer the most recent / highest-version record if the feed carries
    # an ordering column; otherwise pick a fully-populated row with stable
    # tiebreakers so re-runs are idempotent.
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    order_candidates = [
        c for c in df.columns
        if any(k in c.lower() for k in ("updated", "modified", "event_time", "event_ts",
                                        "timestamp", "_ts", "ts", "version", "seq",
                                        "ingest", "load", "batch", "processed"))
        and c not in ("order_id", "amount", "category")
    ]

    order_exprs = []
    for c in order_candidates:
        order_exprs.append(F.col(c).cast("string").desc_nulls_last())

    # Prefer rows that actually have real values, then ordering columns,
    # then fully deterministic tiebreakers on the payload itself.
    ordering = [
        F.col("amount").isNotNull().desc(),
        F.col("category").isNotNull().desc(),
    ] + order_exprs + [
        F.col("amount").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("category").asc_nulls_last(),
    ]

    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(*ordering)

    deduped = (
        df.filter(F.col("order_id").isNotNull())
          .withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
          .filter(F.col("_rn") == 1)
          .drop("_rn")
    )

    gold = deduped.select("order_id", "amount", "category")

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Register temp view under the contract name
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    gold.createOrReplaceTempView("silver_orders")

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Materialize final GOLD output
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    if out_path:
        gold.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(out_path)
    elif out_table:
        gold.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable(out_table)
    else:
        raise RuntimeError("No output location provided (AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH / AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE)")

    print("Run is COMPLETED")
    spark.stop()

        "updated_at", "event_time", "event_ts", "event_timestamp",

    category_col = next((c for c in ["category", "cat", "order_category"] if c in cols), None)
    if amount_col is None:
        raise ValueError(f"No amount column found among {sorted(cols)}")
    if category_col is None:
        raise ValueError(f"No category column found among {sorted(cols)}")

    df = raw

    # Normalize key to string, drop null keys (cannot be a valid keyed row).
    df = df.withColumn("order_id", F.col(key_col).cast("string"))
    df = df.filter(F.col("order_id").isNotNull() & (F.trim(F.col("order_id")) != ""))

    amt_str = F.col(amount_col).cast("string")

    )

    # Category as trimmed string.
    df = df.withColumn("category", F.col(category_col).cast("string"))

    # --- deterministic ordering key (data-only, not read order) ----------------
    order_by = []
    if ts_col is not None:
        # recency wins; nulls treated as oldest so real values beat partials
        order_by.append(F.col(ts_col).cast("string").desc_nulls_last())
    # deterministic tie-breakers on the payload so identical-ts duplicates
    # still collapse to a single, reproducible winner
    order_by.extend([
        F.col("amount").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("category").desc_nulls_last(),
    ])

    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(*order_by)

    deduped = (
        df.withColumn("__rn", F.row_number().over(w))
          .filter(F.col("__rn") == 1)
          .select("order_id", "amount", "category")
    )

    # Contract grain guarantee: exactly one row per order_id.
    deduped = deduped.dropDuplicates(["order_id"])

    # --- write GOLD ------------------------------------------------------------
    deduped.createOrReplaceTempView("silver_orders")

    if OUTPUT_PATH:
        deduped.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(OUTPUT_PATH)
    elif OUTPUT_TABLE:
        deduped.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable(OUTPUT_TABLE)
    else:
        deduped.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(DEFAULT_OUTPUT)

    print("Run is COMPLETED")
    spark.stop()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import os
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

DEFAULT_INPUT = "/home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed31415.ndjson"
DEFAULT_OUTPUT = "/home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/new_merge_upsert__A__seed31415/silver_orders.parquet"


def main():
    spark = (
        SparkSession.builder
        .appName("new_merge_upsert")
        .getOrCreate()
    )

    input_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_INPUT_PATH", DEFAULT_INPUT)
    out_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH", DEFAULT_OUTPUT)
    out_table = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE")

    raw = spark.read.json(input_path)

    # Normalize column lookup case-insensitively to avoid ambiguous refs.
    lower_map = {}
    for c in raw.columns:
        lower_map.setdefault(c.lower(), c)

    def col_or_null(name):
        real = lower_map.get(name)
        return F.col(real) if real is not None else F.lit(None)

    # --- Build canonical, well-typed columns (no reliance on messy inputs) ---
    # order_id: coerce to string, trimmed.
    order_id = F.trim(col_or_null("order_id").cast("string"))

    # amount: strip any currency/thousands junk, then try_cast so bad rows -> NULL
    # instead of blowing up (CAST_INVALID_INPUT).
    amount_str = F.regexp_replace(col_or_null("amount").cast("string"), r"[^0-9eE.+-]", "")
    amount_str = F.nullif(amount_str, F.lit(""))
    amount = F.expr("try_cast(_amt_str as double)")

    # category: plain string, trimmed, blanks -> null
    category = F.nullif(F.trim(col_or_null("category").cast("string")), F.lit(""))

    df = (
        raw
        .withColumn("_order_id", order_id)
        .withColumn("_amt_str", amount_str)
    )
    df = df.withColumn("_amount", amount)
    df = df.withColumn("_category", category)

    # Drop rows with no usable key.
    df = df.filter(F.col("_order_id").isNotNull() & (F.col("_order_id") != F.lit("")))

    # --- Deterministic ordering to pick the order's "real" (latest) values ---
    order_by = [
        F.col("_amount").isNotNull().desc(),   # prefer rows carrying real values
        F.col("_category").isNotNull().desc(),
    ]
    for cand in ["updated_at", "update_ts", "updated", "event_time", "event_ts",
                 "ts", "timestamp", "ingest_ts", "_ingest_ts", "seq",
                 "version", "batch_id", "batch"]:
        real = lower_map.get(cand)
        if real is not None:
            order_by.append(F.col(real).desc_nulls_last())

    # Final deterministic tie-breakers so replays are idempotent.
    order_by.append(F.col("_amount").desc_nulls_last())
    order_by.append(F.col("_category").asc_nulls_last())

    w = Window.partitionBy("_order_id").orderBy(*order_by)
    deduped = (
        df.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
          .filter(F.col("_rn") == 1)
    )

    silver = deduped.select(
        F.col("_order_id").alias("order_id"),
        F.col("_amount").alias("amount"),
        F.col("_category").alias("category"),
    )

    silver.createOrReplaceTempView("silver_orders")

    if out_table:
        silver.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable(out_table)
    else:
        silver.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(out_path)

    print("Run is COMPLETED")
    spark.stop()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

"""
pipeline.py -- idempotent merge/upsert for the messy `orders` feed.

Ticket: nightly load re-reads overlapping windows of the `orders` feed, so the
per-order table keeps growing (same orders re-written, totals creep up). We must
produce `silver_orders` keyed by order_id carrying (amount, category) with
EXACTLY one row per order_id, reflecting the order's real values, and stable
across arbitrarily many overlapping replays.

Strategy:
  * Read NDJSON (schema-on-read, permissive).
  * Normalise: cast amount->double, category->string, coerce order_id.
  * Deduplicate to one row per order_id by picking the *latest* observation
    using whatever ordering signal the feed provides (event-time / version /
    sequence / ingest ts). Ties are broken deterministically so that replaying
    the same input can never change the chosen row (idempotence).
  * Drop rows without a usable order_id.
"""

import os
import sys

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, Window
from pyspark.sql import functions as F
from pyspark.sql.types import DoubleType, StringType


DEFAULT_INPUT = (
    "/home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/"
    ".work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed16180.ndjson"
)


def get_spark():
    return (
        SparkSession.builder
        .appName("new_merge_upsert")
        .master(os.environ.get("SPARK_MASTER", "local[*]"))
        .config("spark.sql.shuffle.partitions", "8")
        .getOrCreate()
    )


def pick_first_present(cols, candidates):
    lower = {c.lower(): c for c in cols}
    for cand in candidates:
        if cand in lower:
            return lower[cand]
    return None


def build_silver(df):
    cols = df.columns

    # --- locate the key column ------------------------------------------------
    key_col = pick_first_present(cols, ["order_id", "orderid", "id", "order"])
    if key_col is None:
        raise ValueError(f"No order_id-like column found in {cols}")

    # --- locate the payload columns ------------------------------------------
    amount_col = pick_first_present(
        cols, ["amount", "amt", "total", "value", "price", "order_amount"]
    )
    category_col = pick_first_present(
        cols, ["category", "cat", "type", "order_category", "segment"]
    )

    # --- locate an ordering signal for "latest wins" -------------------------
    order_col = pick_first_present(
        cols,
        [
            "updated_at", "update_ts", "event_time", "event_ts", "event_timestamp",
            "timestamp", "ts", "modified_at", "modified", "ingested_at",
            "ingest_ts", "_ingested_at", "processing_time", "version", "seq",
            "sequence", "revision", "rev", "batch_id", "created_at",
        ],
    )

    # Normalise types up front.
    df = df.withColumn(key_col, F.col(key_col).cast(StringType()))
    df = df.filter(F.col(key_col).isNotNull() & (F.trim(F.col(key_col)) != ""))

    if amount_col is not None:
        df = df.withColumn("amount", F.col(amount_col).cast(DoubleType()))
    else:
        df = df.withColumn("amount", F.lit(None).cast(DoubleType()))

    if category_col is not None:
        df = df.withColumn("category", F.col(category_col).cast(StringType()))
    else:
        df = df.withColumn("category", F.lit(None).cast(StringType()))

    df = df.withColumnRenamed(key_col, "order_id")

    # --- deterministic latest-wins dedup -------------------------------------
    # Ordering priority:
    #   1. the discovered ordering column (latest first), if any
    #   2. rows that actually carry a non-null amount/category (prefer real data
    #      over blank/corrupt duplicates)
    #   3. a fully deterministic tie-break so identical inputs always pick the
    #      same surviving row -> idempotent on replay.
    order_exprs = []

    if order_col is not None:
        order_exprs.append(F.col(order_col).desc_nulls_last())

    # Prefer rows that have real payload values over empty duplicates.
    order_exprs.append(F.col("amount").isNotNull().desc())
    order_exprs.append(F.col("category").isNotNull().desc())

    # Fully deterministic tie-break across every column (stringified) so that
    # re-running on the same data always resolves to the identical winner.
    tie_break = F.concat_ws(
        "\u0001", *[F.coalesce(F.col(c).cast(StringType()), F.lit("")) for c in df.columns]
    )
    df = df.withColumn("_tie_break", tie_break)
    order_exprs.append(F.col("_tie_break").desc_nulls_last())

    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(*order_exprs)

    deduped = (
        df.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
        .filter(F.col("_rn") == 1)
        .select("order_id", "amount", "category")
    )

    return deduped


def main():
    spark = get_spark()
    spark.sparkContext.setLogLevel("WARN")

    input_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_INPUT_PATH", DEFAULT_INPUT)
    output_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH")
    output_table = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE")

    raw = spark.read.json(input_path)

    # --- lightweight profiling (stderr, does not affect contract) ------------
    sys.stderr.write("=== PROFILE: raw schema ===\n")
    for f in raw.schema.fields:
        sys.stderr.write(f"  {f.name}: {f.dataType.simpleString()}\n")
    total = raw.count()
    sys.stderr.write(f"=== PROFILE: raw row count = {total} ===\n")

    silver = build_silver(raw)

    # Register the contract-named temp view in this SparkSession.
    silver.createOrReplaceTempView("silver_orders")

    distinct_keys = silver.count()
    sys.stderr.write(
        f"=== PROFILE: silver_orders rows (unique order_id) = {distinct_keys} ===\n"
    )

    # Guard invariant: exactly one row per order_id.
    dup = (
        silver.groupBy("order_id").count().filter(F.col("count") > 1).count()
    )
    if dup != 0:
        raise AssertionError(f"invariant violated: {dup} order_ids have >1 row")

    # --- write GOLD output ----------------------------------------------------
    final = silver.select("order_id", "amount", "category")

    if output_path:
        final.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(output_path)
    elif output_table:
        final.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable(output_table)
    else:
        # local fallback next to the module
        final.write.mode("overwrite").parquet("silver_orders.parquet")

    print("Run is COMPLETED")
    spark.stop()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import os
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F, Window

DEFAULT_INPUT = "/home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed14142.ndjson"


def build_silver_orders(df):
    cols = df.columns

    # --- normalize amount: strip currency symbols/commas/whitespace, then cast safely ---
    if "amount" in cols:
        amt_str = F.trim(F.col("amount").cast("string"))
        cleaned = F.regexp_replace(amt_str, r"[^0-9.\-]", "")
        amount_col = F.when((cleaned == "") | cleaned.isNull(), None).otherwise(cleaned).cast("double")
    else:
        amount_col = F.lit(None).cast("double")
    df = df.withColumn("amount", amount_col)

    # --- normalize category ---
    if "category" in cols:
        df = df.withColumn("category", F.col("category").cast("string"))
    else:
        df = df.withColumn("category", F.lit(None).cast("string"))

    # --- normalize key ---
    df = df.withColumn("order_id", F.col("order_id").cast("string"))
    df = df.filter(F.col("order_id").isNotNull())

    # --- deterministic dedup: latest real value per order_id ---
    order_candidates = [
        "updated_at", "update_ts", "modified_at", "event_time", "event_ts",
        "ts", "timestamp", "version", "seq", "ingest_ts", "_ingest_ts", "batch_ts",
    ]
    ordering = [c for c in order_candidates if c in cols]

    order_exprs = [F.col(c).desc_nulls_last() for c in ordering]
    # prefer rows that actually carry a valid amount / category, then deterministic tiebreak
    order_exprs.append(F.col("amount").isNotNull().desc())
    order_exprs.append(F.col("category").isNotNull().desc())
    order_exprs.append(F.col("amount").desc_nulls_last())
    order_exprs.append(F.col("category").asc_nulls_last())

    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(*order_exprs)
    out = (
        df.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
          .filter(F.col("_rn") == 1)
          .drop("_rn")
          .select("order_id", "amount", "category")
    )
    return out


def main():
    spark = (
        SparkSession.builder
        .appName("new_merge_upsert")
        .getOrCreate()
    )

    input_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_INPUT_PATH", DEFAULT_INPUT)

    raw = spark.read.json(input_path)
    silver = build_silver_orders(raw)

    # register contract temp view in the same session
    silver.createOrReplaceTempView("silver_orders")

    out_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH")
    out_table = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE")

    if out_path:
        silver.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(out_path)
    elif out_table:
        silver.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable(out_table)
    else:
        silver.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(
            os.path.join(os.path.dirname(input_path), "silver_orders.parquet")
        )

    print("Run is COMPLETED")
    spark.stop()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, functions as F
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

DATA = (
    "file:///home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/"
    ".work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_messy_orders_seed3141.ndjson"
)


@dp.materialized_view(name="orders_raw")
def orders_raw():
    # Batch relation -> materialized_view (not @dp.table). Unresolved plan only.
    return SparkSession.active().read.json(DATA)


@dp.materialized_view(name="silver_orders")
def silver_orders():
    spark = SparkSession.active()
    raw = spark.read.table("orders_raw")

    # Normalize away formatting messiness so re-emitted copies collapse together.
    norm = raw.select(
        F.col("order_id").alias("order_id"),
        F.col("amount").cast("double").alias("amount"),
        F.trim(F.col("category").cast("string")).alias("category"),
    )

    # Content-based majority vote per order_id. Replay multiplies every variant's
    # count uniformly, so the argmax is invariant under re-runs (idempotent), and
    # the full ordering key makes the choice deterministic.
    counts = norm.groupBy("order_id", "amount", "category").agg(
        F.count(F.lit(1)).alias("_n")
    )
    w = Window.partitionBy("order_id").orderBy(
        F.col("_n").desc(),
        F.col("amount").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("category").asc_nulls_last(),
    )
    ranked = counts.withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))

    # Exactly one row per order_id, carrying the contract columns.
    return ranked.filter(F.col("_rn") == 1).select("order_id", "amount", "category")

SCHEMA = StructType([

        F.col("ts").desc_nulls_last(),

    )

from pyspark.sql.types import (
    StructType, TimestampType, DateType, LongType, IntegerType,
    DoubleType, FloatType, DecimalType, ShortType, ByteType,
)

DEFAULT_INPUT = "/home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_payments_seed42.ndjson"
FX_INPUT = "/home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_fx_rates_cdc_seed42.ndjson"
DEFAULT_OUTPUT = "/home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/.work.powered.AB.n12.part2/new_stream_stream_join__A__seed42/usd_daily.parquet"

_NUMERIC = (LongType, IntegerType, DoubleType, FloatType, DecimalType, ShortType, ByteType)


def is_numeric(df, c):
    return isinstance(df.schema[c].dataType, _NUMERIC)


def flatten(df):
    """Recursively expand top-level struct columns into flat cols."""
    changed = True
    while changed:
        changed = False
        for f in df.schema.fields:
            if isinstance(f.dataType, StructType):
                for n in f.dataType.names:
                    df = df.withColumn("{}_{}".format(f.name, n),
                                       F.col("`{}`.`{}`".format(f.name, n)))
                df = df.drop(f.name)
                changed = True
                break
    return df


def ordered_cols(df):
    """Prefer CDC 'after' images over the raw ordering."""
    cols = df.columns
    return sorted(cols, key=lambda c: (0 if "after" in c.lower() else 1, cols.index(c)))


def pick(df, keywords, numeric=None, exclude=()):
    cols = ordered_cols(df)
    for kw in keywords:
        for c in cols:
            lc = c.lower()
            if any(e in lc for e in exclude):
                continue
            if kw in lc:
                if numeric is True and not is_numeric(df, c):
                    continue
                if numeric is False and is_numeric(df, c):
                    continue
                return c
    return None


def parse_ts(df, colname):
    t = df.schema[colname].dataType
    c = F.col(colname)
    if isinstance(t, TimestampType):
        return c
    if isinstance(t, DateType):
        return c.cast("timestamp")
    if is_numeric(df, colname):
        v = c.cast("double")
        secs = (F.when(v > F.lit(1e14), v / F.lit(1e6))
                 .when(v > F.lit(1e11), v / F.lit(1e3))
                 .otherwise(v))
        return F.to_timestamp(F.from_unixtime(secs))
    # string: rely on Spark's ISO-8601 parsing, fall back to explicit patterns
    return F.coalesce(
        F.to_timestamp(c),
        F.to_timestamp(c, "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss[.SSS][XXX]"),
        F.to_timestamp(c, "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"),
    )


def main():
    spark = (SparkSession.builder
             .appName("stream_stream_asof_fx")
             .config("spark.sql.session.timeZone", "UTC")
             .getOrCreate())
    spark.conf.set("spark.sql.session.timeZone", "UTC")

    in_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_INPUT_PATH", DEFAULT_INPUT)
    fx_path = FX_INPUT

    # ---- read raw feeds ----
    payments = flatten(spark.read.json(in_path))
    rates = flatten(spark.read.json(fx_path))

    # ---- detect payment columns ----
    p_ts = pick(payments,
                ["occurred", "payment_time", "paid_at", "paid", "event_time",
                 "event_ts", "created", "timestamp", "event", "time", "ts", "date"],
                exclude=("update", "settle"))
    p_ccy = pick(payments, ["currency", "ccy", "curr"])
    p_amt = pick(payments, ["amount", "amt", "value", "price"], numeric=True,
                 exclude=("usd", "rate", "id"))
    p_id = pick(payments, ["payment_id", "txn_id", "transaction_id", "event_id", "id"])

    if p_ts is None or p_ccy is None or p_amt is None:
        raise ValueError("Could not detect payment columns: ts=%s ccy=%s amt=%s cols=%s"
                         % (p_ts, p_ccy, p_amt, payments.columns))

    # ---- detect fx rate columns (prefer 'after' image, ignore 'before') ----
    r_val = pick(rates, ["fx_rate", "usd_rate", "rate", "exchange", "fx", "price", "value"],
                 numeric=True, exclude=("time", "ts", "date", "stamp", "before"))
    r_ccy = pick(rates, ["currency", "ccy", "curr"], exclude=("before",))
    r_ts = pick(rates,
                ["rate_time", "rate_ts", "effective", "valid_from", "as_of",
                 "updated", "event_time", "event_ts", "timestamp", "event",
                 "time", "ts", "date"],
                exclude=("before",))

    if r_val is None or r_ccy is None or r_ts is None:
        raise ValueError("Could not detect fx columns: val=%s ccy=%s ts=%s cols=%s"
                         % (r_val, r_ccy, r_ts, rates.columns))

    # ---- optionally drop CDC delete rows ----
    op_col = None
    for c in rates.columns:
        if c.lower() in ("op", "operation", "__op", "cdc_op", "change_type", "_op"):
            op_col = c
            break
    if op_col is not None:
        rates = rates.filter(
            ~F.lower(F.col(op_col).cast("string")).isin("d", "delete", "del", "remove")
            | F.col(op_col).isNull()
        )

    # ---- normalize payments (clean, distinct column names) ----
    if p_id is not None:
        pay_id_expr = F.col(p_id).cast("string")
    else:
        pay_id_expr = F.monotonically_increasing_id().cast("string")

    pay = payments.select(
        pay_id_expr.alias("pay_id"),
        parse_ts(payments, p_ts).alias("pay_ts"),
        F.upper(F.trim(F.col(p_ccy).cast("string"))).alias("pay_ccy"),
        F.col(p_amt).cast("double").alias("pay_amount"),
    ).filter(F.col("pay_ts").isNotNull())

    # give every row a guaranteed-unique key for the point-in-time window
    pay = pay.withColumn("pay_key", F.monotonically_increasing_id())

    # ---- normalize rates ----
    rte = rates.select(
        F.upper(F.trim(F.col(r_ccy).cast("string"))).alias("rate_ccy"),
        F.col(r_val).cast("double").alias("rate_val"),
        parse_ts(rates, r_ts).alias("rate_ts"),
    ).filter(F.col("rate_ts").isNotNull() & F.col("rate_val").isNotNull())

    # ---- as-of join: latest rate with rate_ts <= pay_ts, same currency ----
    cond = (F.col("pay_ccy") == F.col("rate_ccy")) & (F.col("rate_ts") <= F.col("pay_ts"))
    joined = pay.join(rte, cond, "left")

    w = Window.partitionBy("pay_key").orderBy(
        F.col("rate_ts").desc_nulls_last(),
        F.col("rate_val").desc_nulls_last(),
    )
    picked = (joined
              .withColumn("_rn", F.row_number().over(w))
              .filter(F.col("_rn") == 1)
              .drop("_rn"))

    # ---- enrich: USD value + carried as-applied rate timestamp ----
    enriched = (picked
                .withColumn("usd_amount", F.col("pay_amount") * F.col("rate_val"))
                .withColumnRenamed("rate_ts", "rate_ts_applied")
                .withColumn("settlement_date", F.to_date(F.col("pay_ts")))
                .select(
                    "pay_id",
                    "pay_ts",
                    "pay_ccy",
                    "pay_amount",
                    "rate_val",
                    "rate_ts_applied",   # as-applied rate timestamp (audit)
                    "usd_amount",
                    "settlement_date",
                ))

    enriched.createOrReplaceTempView("enriched_payments")

    # ---- gold: USD per UTC calendar day ----
    usd_daily = (enriched
                 .groupBy("settlement_date")
                 .agg(F.sum("usd_amount").alias("usd_total"))
                 .select(
                     F.col("settlement_date").cast(DateType()).alias("settlement_date"),
                     F.col("usd_total").cast("double").alias("usd_total"),
                 )
                 .orderBy("settlement_date"))

    usd_daily.createOrReplaceTempView("usd_daily")

    # ---- write gold ----
    out_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH")
    out_table = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE")
    if out_path:
        usd_daily.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(out_path)
    elif out_table:
        usd_daily.write.mode("overwrite").saveAsTable(out_table)
    else:
        usd_daily.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(DEFAULT_OUTPUT)

    print("Run is COMPLETED")
    spark.stop()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

"""
pipeline.py -- Stream/stream point-in-time (as-of) FX enrichment.

Ticket: convert every payment to USD using the FX rate that was in effect at the
moment the payment occurred (rate_ts <= payment_time, latest such rate), carry the
as-applied rate timestamp through for audit, and report USD per UTC calendar day.

Correctness guarantees:
  * never uses a rate stamped AFTER the payment (strict <= on rate_ts);
  * deterministic tie-breaking so reprocessing the same input is stable;
  * foreign value is preserved on the enriched rows (amount + currency kept).
"""

import os
import sys

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, Window
from pyspark.sql import functions as F


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Defensive schema detection helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def _find(cols, candidates):
    lc = {c.lower(): c for c in cols}
    for cand in candidates:
        if cand.lower() in lc:
            return lc[cand.lower()]
    # loose contains match
    for cand in candidates:
        for c in cols:
            if cand.lower() in c.lower():
                return c
    return None


def _flatten_cdc(df):
    """Some CDC feeds nest the payload under an 'after' (and 'before') struct."""
    cols = df.columns
    if "after" in [c.lower() for c in cols]:
        after_col = _find(cols, ["after"])
        # promote struct fields to top level, keep any top-level meta columns too
        struct_fields = [f.name for f in df.schema[after_col].dataType.fields]
        sel = [F.col(after_col + "." + f).alias(f) for f in struct_fields]
        # keep top-level timestamp-ish/op columns if present
        for c in cols:
            if c != after_col and c.lower() not in [s.lower() for s in struct_fields]:
                sel.append(F.col(c))
        return df.select(*sel)
    return df


def _parse_ts(df, col):
    dt = dict(df.dtypes)[col]
    c = F.col(col)
    if dt == "timestamp":
        return c
    if dt in ("bigint", "int", "long", "smallint", "double", "float", "decimal"):
        lc = c.cast("long")
        # heuristics: > 1e12 -> millis, else seconds
        return F.when(lc > F.lit(100000000000), F.timestamp_millis(lc)).otherwise(
            F.timestamp_seconds(lc)
        )
    # string forms (ISO-8601 with T/Z, or space-separated)
    return F.coalesce(F.to_timestamp(c), c.cast("timestamp"))


def _need(name, col, where):
    if col is None:
        raise ValueError("Could not locate a '%s' column in %s" % (name, where))
    return col


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def main():
    default_payments = (
        "/home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/"
        ".work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_payments_seed1337.ndjson"
    )
    default_fx = (
        "/home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/"
        ".work.powered.AB.n12.part2/_data/gen_fx_rates_cdc_seed1337.ndjson"
    )
    default_out = (
        "/home/lnc/repos/ssa-powered-run/experiments/safe_agent_study/"
        ".work.powered.AB.n12.part2/new_stream_stream_join__A__seed1337/usd_daily.parquet"
    )

    payments_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_INPUT_PATH", default_payments)
    fx_path = os.environ.get("FX_RATES_CDC_PATH", default_fx)
    out_path = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_PATH")
    out_table = os.environ.get("AGENT_OUTPUT_TABLE")
    if not out_path and not out_table:
        out_path = default_out

    spark = (
        SparkSession.builder.appName("stream_stream_asof_fx")
        .config("spark.sql.session.timeZone", "UTC")
        .getOrCreate()
    )
    spark.conf.set("spark.sql.session.timeZone", "UTC")

    # -------------------- read --------------------
    payments = spark.read.json(payments_path)
    fx = spark.read.json(fx_path)
    fx = _flatten_cdc(fx)

    # -------------------- detect payment columns --------------------
    pcols = payments.columns
    p_amount = _need(
        "amount",
        _find(pcols, ["amount", "foreign_amount", "amount_foreign", "value",
                      "payment_amount", "amt", "orig_amount"]),
        "payments",
    )
    p_ccy = _need(
        "currency",
        _find(pcols, ["currency", "currency_code", "ccy", "curr", "iso_currency"]),
        "payments",
    )
    p_time = _need(
        "event_time",
        _find(pcols, ["event_time", "event_ts", "payment_time", "payment_ts",
                      "occurred_at", "created_at", "timestamp", "ts", "time"]),
        "payments",
    )
    p_id = _find(pcols, ["payment_id", "id", "txn_id", "transaction_id", "pk"])

    # -------------------- detect fx columns --------------------
    fcols = fx.columns
    f_rate = _need(
        "rate",
        _find(fcols, ["rate", "fx_rate", "usd_rate", "rate_to_usd", "exchange_rate",
                      "conversion_rate", "price"]),
        "fx_rates_cdc",
    )
    f_ccy = _need(
        "currency",
        _find(fcols, ["currency", "currency_code", "ccy", "curr", "iso_currency"]),
        "fx_rates_cdc",
    )
    f_time = _need(
        "rate_ts",
        _find(fcols, ["rate_ts", "rate_time", "effective_ts", "effective_time",
                      "valid_from", "as_of", "event_time", "event_ts",
                      "timestamp", "ts", "time"]),
        "fx_rates_cdc",
    )

    # -------------------- normalize --------------------
    payments_n = payments.select(
        (F.col(p_id).cast("string") if p_id else F.monotonically_increasing_id().cast("string")).alias("payment_id"),
        F.upper(F.trim(F.col(p_ccy).cast("string"))).alias("currency"),
        F.col(p_amount).cast("double").alias("foreign_amount"),
        _parse_ts(payments, p_time).alias("event_time"),
    )
    # ensure a stable unique id even if source id has duplicates: not strictly needed,
    # but keep as provided.

    fx_n = (
        fx.select(
            F.upper(F.trim(F.col(f_ccy).cast("string"))).alias("currency"),
            F.col(f_rate).cast("double").alias("rate"),
            _parse_ts(fx, f_time).alias("rate_ts"),
        )
